- **dont** — *whose / of which* (advanced, but good to recognize)
- **ce qui / ce que** — *what (subject) / what (object)* (e.g., *Ce que je veux...* = What I want...)
- **la personne qui... / le chose que...** — *the person who... / the thing that...*
- **C'est exactement ce que je voulais dire !** — *That's exactly what I meant to say!*
- **Je comprends ce que tu dis** — *I understand what you're saying*

quizPairs.question: C'est... qui / C'est... que
quizPairs.answer: It is... who / It is... that

quizPairs.question: Placement & agreement
quizPairs.answer: que becomes qu' before a vowel

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 38: Polite Requests & Conditional Politeness

Welcome to Lesson 38! Today you'll learn how to make polite requests using the conditional mood — essential for formal situations, customer service, and showing respect in French.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je voudrais... / Nous voudrions...
> *I would like... / We would like...*
> Pronounced: zhuh voo-dreh / noo voo-dree-yohn. The conditional of *vouloir* — very polite.

### Pourrais-je... ? / Pourrions-nous... ?
> *Could I...? / Could we...?*
> Pronounced: poo-rehzh / poo-ree-ohn noo. Formal question form using conditional.

### Auriez-vous... ? / Aurais-tu... ?
> *Would you have...? (formal / informal)*
> Pronounced: oh-ryay voo / oh-reh tew. Conditional of *avoir* for polite requests.

### Ce serait possible de... ?
> *Would it be possible to...?*
> Pronounced: suh suh-reh poh-seebl duh. Very polite way to ask for something.

### Je serais reconnaissant(e) si...
> *I would be grateful if...*
> Pronounced: zhuh suh-reh ruh-koh-neh-sahNt see. Formal expression of gratitude in advance.

### Pourriez-vous m'aider / me dire / me montrer... ?
> *Could you help me / tell me / show me...?*
> Pronounced: poo-ryay voo may-day / muh deer / muh mohn-tray. Essential polite requests.

### Merci d'avance / Je vous remercie
> *Thank you in advance / I thank you*
> Pronounced: mehr-see dah-vahns / zhuh voo ruh-mer-see. Closing polite requests gracefully.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Conditional for Politeness

💡 The conditional mood softens requests and sounds more polite:

| Present (direct) | Conditional (polite) | English |
|-----------------|---------------------|---------|
| Je veux... | Je voudrais... | I want → I would like |
| Tu peux... | Tu pourrais... | You can → You could |
| Vous avez... | Vous auriez... | You have → Would you have |
| C'est possible... | Ce serait possible... | It's possible → Would it be possible |

🗣️ Tip: Use conditional + *s'il vous plaît* for maximum politeness!

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **Je me permets de vous demander...** — *I take the liberty of asking you...* (very formal)
- **Serait-il possible de... ?** — *Would it be possible to...?* (impersonal polite form)
- **Je vous saurais gré de...** — *I would be grateful if you would...* (highly formal)
- **Dans la mesure du possible** — *If possible / As much as possible*
- **Je reste à votre disposition** — *I remain at your disposal* (professional closing)

quizPairs.question: Pourriez-vous m'aider / me dire / me montrer... ?
quizPairs.answer: Could you help me / tell me / show me...?

quizPairs.question: Merci d'avance / Je vous remercie
quizPairs.answer: Thank you in advance / I thank you

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 39: Final Comprehensive Review

Welcome to Lesson 39 — your final review before graduation! Today we'll consolidate all key grammar and vocabulary from the entire Beginner course to ensure you're ready for real-world French.

---

## 🔤 Review: Core Grammar Pillars

### Present Tense & -er Verbs
- Je parle / Tu manges / Il travaille — I speak / You eat / He works
- Regular pattern: drop -er, add -e, -es, -e, -ons, -ez, -ent

### Past Tense (Passé Composé)
- J'ai parlé / J'ai mangé / Je suis allé(e) — I spoke / I ate / I went
- Avoir + participle OR Être + participle (for movement verbs)

### Near Future (Futur Proche)
- Je vais + infinitive — I am going to...
- Example: Je vais visiter Paris. I'm going to visit Paris.

### Negation Patterns
- ne...pas / ne...jamais / ne...plus / ne...rien — not / never / no longer / nothing
- Example: Je ne mange jamais de viande. I never eat meat.

### Object Pronouns
- le/la/les (direct) / lui/leur (indirect) — him/her/it/them
- Placement: before the verb (except affirmative commands)

### Relative Pronouns
- qui (subject) / que (object) / où (place/time)
- Example: La personne qui parle / Le livre que je lis / La ville où j'habite

### Politeness & Conditional
- Je voudrais... / Pourriez-vous...? — I would like... / Could you...?
- Essential for formal situations and respectful communication

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Your Beginner Toolkit

💡 You now have the tools to:

✅ Introduce yourself and talk about your life
✅ Ask and answer questions about past, present, and future
✅ Navigate travel, shopping, dining, and health situations
✅ Express emotions, preferences, and relationships
✅ Make polite requests and understand French culture

🗣️ Final tip: Focus on communication, not perfection. Mistakes are part of learning!

---

## 🎁 Bonus: Ready for Real Life

- **Je peux me débrouiller en français !** — *I can get by in French!*
- **Je comprends l'essentiel** — *I understand the essentials*
- **Je pose des questions quand je ne comprends pas** — *I ask questions when I don't understand*
- **Je pratique un peu chaque jour** — *I practice a little every day*
- **Je suis prêt(e) pour le niveau suivant !** — *I'm ready for the next level!*

quizPairs.answer: I ask questions when I don't understand

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 1: Childhood & Habits — Introduction to Imparfait

Welcome to **Coffee Break French A2**! You've completed the Beginner level — congratulations! Now we'll dive deeper into the French past. In this lesson, you'll discover the **imparfait** (imperfect tense), used for habits, descriptions, and ongoing actions in the past.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Quand j'étais enfant
> *When I was a child*
> Pronounced: kahn zhay-teh ahn-fahn. The imparfait describes background or habitual situations.

### Je jouais souvent au parc
> *I often played at the park*
> Pronounced: zhuh zhoo-eh soo-vahn oh park. Habitual past action.

### Il faisait beau
> *The weather was nice*
> Pronounced: eel fuh-zeh boh. Describing atmosphere or weather in the past.

### Nous habitions à Lyon
> *We used to live in Lyon*
> Pronounced: noo zah-bee-tyohn ah lee-ohn. Habitual or ongoing situation.

### Tu mangeais quoi ?
> *What did you use to eat?*
> Pronounced: too mahn-zhay kwah. Asking about past habits.

### Elle était contente
> *She was happy*
> Pronounced: ell ay-teh kohn-tahnt. Describing a state or feeling in the past.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Imparfait Formation (Regular -er Verbs)

To form the imparfait, take the **nous** form of the present tense, drop **-ons**, and add the imparfait endings:

| Subject | Ending | Example (jouer) |
|---------|--------|-----------------|
| je | -ais | je jouais |
| tu | -ais | tu jouais |
| il/elle/on | -ait | il jouait |
| nous | -ions | nous jouions |
| vous | -iez | vous jouiez |
| ils/elles | -aient | ils jouaient |

💡 **Tip**: The stem is always regular — no stem changes in the imparfait!

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **autrefois** — *in the past / formerly*
- **à l'époque** — *back then / at that time*
- **tous les jours** — *every day* (with past: *Je mangeais des croissants tous les jours*)
- **souvent / rarement** — *often / rarely*
- **Quand j'avais dix ans** — *When I was ten years old*

quizPairs.question: Je jouais souvent au parc
quizPairs.answer: I often played at the park

quizPairs.question: Nous habitions à Lyon
quizPairs.answer: We used to live in Lyon

quizPairs.question: Tu mangeais quoi ?
quizPairs.answer: What did you use to eat?

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 2: Describing the Past — Imparfait with être & avoir

Welcome back! In this lesson, you'll learn to use **être** and **avoir** in the imparfait — two essential verbs for describing the past. You'll be able to talk about how people or places were and what they had.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### J'étais fatigué
> *I was tired (masculine)*
> Pronounced: zhay-teh fah-tee-gay. *Être* in imparfait: j'étais, tu étais, il/elle était.

### Il avait une grande maison
> *He had a big house*
> Pronounced: eel ah-veh oon grahnd may-zohn. *Avoir* in imparfait: j'avais, tu avais, il/elle avait.

### C'était magnifique
> *It was magnificent*
> Pronounced: say-teh mah-nyee-feek. *C'était* = "it was" — very common in descriptions.

### Nous étions à la plage
> *We were at the beach*
> Pronounced: noo zay-tyohn ah lah plazh. Describing location in the past.

### Tu avais raison
> *You were right*
> Pronounced: too ah-veh reh-zohn. Idiom: *avoir raison* = to be right.

### Elles étaient contentes
> *They (f) were happy*
> Pronounced: ell zay-teh kohn-tahnt. Note: *être* agrees with the subject (étai**ent** for elles).

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: être and avoir in Imparfait

| Verb | je | tu | il/elle | nous | vous | ils/elles |
|------|-----|-----|---------|------|------|-----------|
| être | j'étais | tu étais | il était | nous étions | vous étiez | ils étaient |
| avoir | j'avais | tu avais | il avait | nous avions | vous aviez | ils avaient |

💡 **Tip**: The imparfait of être and avoir is irregular — memorize these forms. They appear constantly!

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **avoir faim / avoir soif** — *to be hungry / thirsty* (e.g., *J'avais faim*)
- **avoir peur** — *to be scared* (*Il avait peur du noir*)
- **être en retard** — *to be late* (*Elle était en retard*)
- **C'était bien** — *It was good*
- **Il n'y avait pas** — *There wasn't / weren't*

## Lesson 3: Past Routines — Imparfait for Continuing & Habitual Actions

Welcome back! The imparfait is perfect for talking about what *used to happen* or what *was happening* over time. In this lesson, you'll master ongoing actions and repeated habits in the past.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je lisais tous les soirs
> *I used to read every evening*
> Pronounced: zhuh lee-zeh too lay swar. Habitual action — something that happened repeatedly.

### Il regardait la télé quand...
> *He was watching TV when...*
> Pronounced: eel ruh-gar-deh lah tay-lay kahn. Ongoing action interrupted by another event.

### On allait au cinéma chaque semaine
> *We used to go to the cinema every week*
> Pronounced: ohn ah-leh oh see-nay-mah shahk suh-men. Habit with frequency.

### Elle travaillait pendant que...
> *She was working while...*
> Pronounced: ell trah-vy-eh pahn-dahn kuh. Two simultaneous past actions — both imparfait.

### Vous mangiez à quelle heure ?
> *What time did you used to eat?*
> Pronounced: voo mahn-zhyay ah kel ur. Asking about past routines.

### Ils faisaient du sport
> *They used to do sports*
> Pronounced: eel fuh-zeh doo sport. General habitual activity.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Imparfait Uses

| Use | Example |
|-----|---------|
| Habit | *Je buvais du café le matin* (I used to drink coffee in the morning) |
| Ongoing action | *Il lisait quand le téléphone a sonné* (He was reading when the phone rang) |
| Description | *Le ciel était bleu* (The sky was blue) |
| Simultaneous actions | *Elle chantait pendant qu'il jouait* (She sang while he played) |

💡 **Tip**: Imparfait = background. Passé composé = specific event. More on that in Lesson 6!

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **d'habitude** — *usually* (*D'habitude, je me levais à sept heures*)
- **tous les jours / toutes les semaines** — *every day / every week*
- **de temps en temps** — *from time to time*
- **pendant que** — *while*
- **quand j'étais jeune** — *when I was young*

quizPairs.question: Je lisais tous les soirs
quizPairs.answer: I used to read every evening

quizPairs.question: On allait au cinéma chaque semaine
quizPairs.answer: We used to go to the cinema every week

quizPairs.question: Vous mangiez à quelle heure ?
quizPairs.answer: What time did you used to eat?

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 4: Weather & Atmosphere — Imparfait with il faisait, c'était

Welcome back! Weather and atmosphere are often described in the imparfait — they set the scene. In this lesson, you'll learn to talk about the weather and mood of past moments.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Il faisait beau / mauvais
> *The weather was nice / bad*
> Pronounced: eel fuh-zeh boh / moh-veh. *Faire* in imparfait for weather: *il faisait*.

### Il faisait froid / chaud
> *It was cold / hot*
> Pronounced: eel fuh-zeh frwah / shoh.

### Il pleuvait / il neigeait
> *It was raining / it was snowing*
> Pronounced: eel pluh-veh / eel neh-zhay. Impersonal *il* + verb in imparfait.

### C'était une belle journée
> *It was a beautiful day*
> Pronounced: say-teh oon bell zhoor-nay. General appreciation of a moment.

### Le ciel était bleu
> *The sky was blue*
> Pronounced: luh syel ay-teh bluh. Description with *être*.

### On se sentait bien
> *We felt good*
> Pronounced: ohn suh sahn-teh byan. Reflexive verb *se sentir* in imparfait.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Weather in Imparfait

| Expression | Meaning |
|------------|---------|
| Il faisait beau | It was nice (weather) |
| Il faisait mauvais | It was bad (weather) |
| Il faisait froid / chaud | It was cold / hot |
| Il pleuvait | It was raining |
| Il neigeait | It was snowing |
| Il y avait du soleil | There was sun |
| Il y avait du vent | There was wind |

💡 **Tip**: Use *il faisait* for temperature/conditions; *il* + verb for rain/snow.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il y avait** — *there was / were* (imparfait of *il y a*)
- **une journée ensoleillée** — *a sunny day*
- **un temps gris** — *grey / overcast weather*
- **C'était agréable** — *It was pleasant*
- **L'atmosphère était...** — *The atmosphere was...*

quizPairs.question: Il pleuvait / il neigeait
quizPairs.answer: It was raining / it was snowing

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 5: Review — Imparfait Consolidation

Welcome back! This is your first A2 review lesson. We'll consolidate everything you've learned about the imparfait: formation, être/avoir, habits, ongoing actions, and weather. Let's put it all together in a real conversation.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases (Recap)

### Quand j'étais petit(e)
> *When I was little*
> Combines time expression + être in imparfait.

### Je regardais la télé tous les soirs
> *I used to watch TV every evening*
> Habit with frequency.

### C'était génial
> *It was great*
> General appreciation.

### Il y avait du soleil
> *There was sun*
> *Il y avait* = there was/were (imparfait).

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Imparfait Quick Reference

| Use | Example |
|-----|---------|
| Habit | *Je jouais au tennis* |
| Ongoing | *Elle lisait quand...* |
| Description | *Il faisait beau* |
| être/avoir | *J'étais content. J'avais faim.* |

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **à l'époque** — *back then*
- **autrefois** — *in the old days*
- **d'habitude** — *usually*
- **tous les matins** — *every morning*

quizPairs.question: Je regardais la télé tous les soirs
quizPairs.answer: I used to watch TV every evening

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 6: Passé Composé vs Imparfait — Basics

Welcome back! Now comes a crucial skill: knowing *when* to use passé composé and *when* to use imparfait. In French, we often use both in the same story. Here's the key difference.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je lisais quand le téléphone a sonné
> *I was reading when the phone rang*
> Imparfait (background) + Passé composé (specific event).

### Il faisait beau quand nous sommes arrivés
> *The weather was nice when we arrived*
> Background (imparfait) + completed action (passé composé).

### J'habitais à Paris quand j'ai rencontré Marie
> *I was living in Paris when I met Marie*
> Ongoing situation + specific event.

### Elle avait faim, alors elle a mangé
> *She was hungry, so she ate*
> State (imparfait) + action (passé composé).

### Nous étions au restaurant quand il a commencé à pleuvoir
> *We were at the restaurant when it started to rain*
> Location/state + sudden event.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: When to Use Which?

| Use | Tense | Example |
|-----|-------|---------|
| Specific completed action | Passé composé | *J'ai mangé* (I ate) |
| Background, habit, description | Imparfait | *Je mangeais* (I was eating / used to eat) |
| Ongoing action (interrupted) | Imparfait | *Il lisait quand...* |
| The event that interrupts | Passé composé | *...quand le téléphone a sonné* |

💡 **Tip**: Imparfait = the *frame*. Passé composé = the *action* inside the frame.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **soudain** — *suddenly* (often before passé composé)
- **tout à coup** — *all of a sudden*
- **alors** — *so / then*
- **quand** — *when* (can introduce either tense)
- **pendant que** — *while* (usually imparfait + imparfait)

quizPairs.question: Je lisais quand le téléphone a sonné
quizPairs.answer: I was reading when the phone rang

quizPairs.question: Il faisait beau quand nous sommes arrivés
quizPairs.answer: The weather was nice when we arrived

quizPairs.question: J'habitais à Paris quand j'ai rencontré Marie
quizPairs.answer: I was living in Paris when I met Marie

quizPairs.question: Elle avait faim, alors elle a mangé
quizPairs.answer: She was hungry, so she ate

quizPairs.question: Nous étions au restaurant quand il a commencé à pleuvoir
quizPairs.answer: We were at the restaurant when it started to rain

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 7: Telling a Story — PC for Events + Imparfait for Context

Welcome back! In real French, we weave passé composé and imparfait together to tell stories. The imparfait sets the scene; the passé composé moves the plot. Let's practice storytelling.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Hier soir, j'étais chez moi. J'ai reçu un appel
> *Last night, I was at home. I got a call*
> Imparfait (setting) then passé composé (what happened).

### Il pleuvait. Nous avons décidé de rester
> *It was raining. We decided to stay*
> Description + decision/action.

### Pendant que tu cuisinais, j'ai mis la table
> *While you were cooking, I set the table*
> Simultaneous: ongoing (imparfait) + completed (passé composé).

### Le train partait à 8h. Il a eu un retard
> *The train was leaving at 8. It was delayed*
> Schedule/plan (imparfait) + what actually happened (passé composé).

### C'était génial. On a beaucoup ri
> *It was great. We laughed a lot*
> General feeling + specific action.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Storytelling Structure

1. **Set the scene** (imparfait): *Il faisait beau. Nous étions à la plage.*
2. **Introduce events** (passé composé): *Soudain, j'ai vu un dauphin. J'ai crié.*
3. **Return to background** (imparfait): *Le soleil brillait. C'était magnifique.*

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **d'abord** — *first*
- **ensuite** — *then*
- **finalement** — *finally*
- **tout à coup** — *all of a sudden*
- **en fait** — *actually / in fact*

quizPairs.question: Hier soir, j'étais chez moi. J'ai reçu un appel
quizPairs.answer: Last night, I was at home. I got a call

quizPairs.question: Il pleuvait. Nous avons décidé de rester
quizPairs.answer: It was raining. We decided to stay

quizPairs.question: Pendant que tu cuisinais, j'ai mis la table
quizPairs.answer: While you were cooking, I set the table

quizPairs.question: Le train partait à 8h. Il a eu un retard
quizPairs.answer: The train was leaving at 8. It was delayed

quizPairs.question: C'était génial. On a beaucoup ri
quizPairs.answer: It was great. We laughed a lot

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 8: Interrupted Actions — Imparfait + quand/soudain + PC

Welcome back! When one action interrupts another, the ongoing action is in imparfait and the interrupting action is in passé composé. *Quand* and *soudain* often introduce the interrupt.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je dormais quand l'alarme a sonné
> *I was sleeping when the alarm went off*
> Ongoing (imparfait) interrupted by event (passé composé).

### Il marchait dans la rue quand il a vu Sophie
> *He was walking down the street when he saw Sophie*
> Pronounced: eel mar-sheh dahn lah roo kahn eel ah vyoo so-fee.

### Nous parlions quand le professeur est entré
> *We were talking when the teacher came in*
> Background conversation + entrance.

### Elle faisait la cuisine quand le téléphone a sonné
> *She was cooking when the phone rang*
> *Faire la cuisine* = to cook.

### Soudain, j'ai entendu un bruit
> *Suddenly, I heard a noise*
> *Soudain* + passé composé for the sudden event.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Interrupted Action Formula

**Imparfait** (ongoing) + **quand/soudain** + **Passé composé** (interrupting event)

| Ongoing | Interrupt |
|---------|-----------|
| Je lisais | quand il a frappé à la porte |
| Elle chantait | quand la porte s'est ouverte |
| On parlait | soudain, le train s'est arrêté |

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **entendre** — *to hear* (*J'ai entendu*)
- **voir** — *to see* (*J'ai vu*)
- **frapper à la porte** — *to knock on the door*
- **s'ouvrir** — *to open* (reflexive)
- **se passer** — *to happen* (*Qu'est-ce qui s'est passé?*)

quizPairs.question: Je dormais quand l'alarme a sonné
quizPairs.answer: I was sleeping when the alarm went off

quizPairs.question: Nous parlions quand le professeur est entré
quizPairs.answer: We were talking when the teacher came in

quizPairs.question: Elle faisait la cuisine quand le téléphone a sonné
quizPairs.answer: She was cooking when the phone rang

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 9: Opinions & Descriptions in the Past — Je pensais que...

Welcome back! We use the imparfait for opinions, thoughts, and feelings in the past — what we *thought* or *believed* at the time. This adds depth to your stories.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je pensais que c'était facile
> *I thought it was easy*
> *Penser* in imparfait for past opinion. Pronounced: zhuh pahn-seh kuh say-teh fah-seel.

### Il croyait que tu viendrais
> *He believed you would come*
> *Croire* in imparfait for past belief.

### Elle voulait partir tôt
> *She wanted to leave early*
> *Vouloir* in imparfait for past desire.

### Nous espérions qu'il ferait beau
> *We hoped the weather would be nice*
> *Espérer* in imparfait for past hope.

### C'était difficile, mais j'aimais ça
> *It was difficult, but I liked it*
> Description + past preference.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Verbs of Thought/Feeling in Imparfait

| Verb | Imparfait | Use |
|------|-----------|-----|
| penser | je pensais | I thought |
| croire | je croyais | I believed |
| vouloir | je voulais | I wanted |
| espérer | j'espérais | I hoped |
| aimer | j'aimais | I liked |

💡 **Tip**: These describe inner states — use imparfait, not passé composé.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **je ne savais pas** — *I didn't know*
- **il me semblait que** — *it seemed to me that*
- **j'avais l'impression que** — *I had the impression that*
- **en fait** — *actually* (to correct a past belief)
- **finalement** — *in the end*

quizPairs.question: Nous espérions qu'il ferait beau
quizPairs.answer: We hoped the weather would be nice

quizPairs.question: C'était difficile, mais j'aimais ça
quizPairs.answer: It was difficult, but I liked it

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 10: Review — Mixed Past Tenses

Welcome back! This review brings together everything from Lessons 6 to 9: when to use passé composé vs imparfait, storytelling, interrupted actions, and past opinions. Let's practice it all in one dialogue.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases (Recap)

### Il lisait quand j'ai frappé à la porte
> *He was reading when I knocked on the door*
> Classic interrupted action.

### Je croyais que tu étais parti
> *I thought you had left*
> Past belief (imparfait).

### Nous étions au café quand il a commencé à pleuvoir
> *We were at the café when it started to rain*
> Location + weather event.

### C'était bien. On a passé un bon moment
> *It was good. We had a good time*
> General feeling + specific outcome.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Quick Decision Guide

- **Specific completed action?** → Passé composé
- **Background, habit, description, opinion?** → Imparfait
- **Ongoing + interrupted?** → Imparfait + quand + Passé composé

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **passer un bon moment** — *to have a good time*
- **raconter** — *to tell* (a story)
- **en fait** — *actually*
- **finalement** — *in the end*

quizPairs.question: Il lisait quand j'ai frappé à la porte
quizPairs.answer: He was reading when I knocked on the door

quizPairs.question: Je croyais que tu étais parti
C'était bien. On a passé un bon moment
quizPairs.answer: It was good. We had a good time

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 11: The Pronoun "y" — There, Replacing à + Place

Welcome back! The pronoun **y** replaces phrases with *à* + place. It means "there" or "to it". It's small but powerful — and very common in French.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Tu vas au cinéma ? — Oui, j'y vais
> *Are you going to the cinema? — Yes, I'm going there*
> *Y* replaces *au cinéma* (à + le cinéma). Pronounced: zhee vay.

### Nous y sommes
> *We're there*
> *Y* = there. *Nous y sommes* = We are there.

### Vas-y !
> *Go (ahead)! / Go for it!*
> Imperative: verb + *y*. Pronounced: vah-zee.

### Il pense à son travail. Il y pense tout le temps
> *He thinks about his work. He thinks about it all the time*
> *Y* replaces *à son travail*.

### J'y crois
> *I believe (in it)*
> *Croire à* → *j'y crois*. Pronounced: zhee krwah.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: When to Use "y"

| Replaces | Example | With y |
|----------|---------|--------|
| à + place | *Je vais à Paris* | *J'y vais* |
| à + thing | *Je pense à ça* | *J'y pense* |
| là | (there) | *Nous y sommes* |

**Position**: *y* comes before the verb (or before *en*). *J'y vais. Tu y penses?*

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **Allez-y !** — *Go ahead!*
- **On y va !** — *Let's go!*
- **Il faut y aller** — *We have to go*
- **s'y habituer** — *to get used to it*
- **y réfléchir** — *to think about it*

quizPairs.question: Tu vas au cinéma ? — Oui, j'y vais
quizPairs.answer: Are you going to the cinema? — Yes, I'm going there

quizPairs.question: Il pense à son travail. Il y pense tout le temps
quizPairs.answer: He thinks about his work. He thinks about it all the time

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 12: The Pronoun "en" — With Quantities

Welcome back! The pronoun **en** replaces phrases with *de* + thing, especially quantities. "How many do you have?" — "I have two." In French: *J'en ai deux.*

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Tu veux du café ? — Oui, j'en veux
> *Do you want some coffee? — Yes, I want some*
> *En* replaces *du café* (de + le café). Pronounced: zhuhn vuh.

### J'en ai deux
> *I have two (of them)*
> *En* replaces the noun. Pronounced: zhuhn ay duh.

### Il y en a beaucoup
> *There are a lot (of them)*
> *Il y a* + *en* = *il y en a*.

### Tu en prends ?
> *Do you take some?*
> *Prendre de* → *en prendre*. Pronounced: too ahn prahn.

### Nous n'en avons pas
> *We don't have any*
> *En* + negation. Pronounced: noo nahn ah-vohn pah.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: When to Use "en"

| Replaces | Example | With en |
|----------|---------|---------|
| de + noun (quantity) | *J'ai deux pommes* | *J'en ai deux* |
| partitive (some) | *Je veux du pain* | *J'en veux* |
| de + place (from) | *Je viens de Paris* | *J'en viens* |

**Position**: *en* comes before the verb, after *y* if both: *J'y en mets.* (rare)

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **combien en voulez-vous ?** — *how many do you want?*
- **j'en ai assez** — *I have enough*
- **il n'y en a plus** — *there's none left*
- **s'en aller** — *to leave*
- **en vouloir** — *to want some*

quizPairs.question: Tu veux du café ? — Oui, j'en veux
quizPairs.answer: Do you want some coffee? — Yes, I want some

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 13: "y" and "en" Together

Welcome back! Sometimes we need both **y** and **en** in the same sentence. The order is fixed: **y** comes before **en**. Both go before the verb.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### J'y vais. J'en reviens
> *I'm going there. I'm coming back from there*
> Two separate sentences — each pronoun in its place.

### Il y en a
> *There are some (of them)*
> *Il y a* + *en*: *il y en a*. Pronounced: eel ee ahn ah.

### Tu m'en donnes ? / Tu m'y emmènes ?
> *Can you give me some? / Can you take me there?*
> Pronoun order: me + y/en + verb. *M'en* = me + en.

### On en parle ? — Oui, j'y pense
> *Shall we talk about it? — Yes, I'm thinking about it*
> *En* = about it (de ça). *Y* = to it (à ça). Different verbs, different prepositions.

### Il faut y aller. J'en ai marre
> *We have to go. I'm fed up with it*
> *Y aller* = to go (there). *En avoir marre* = to be fed up.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Pronoun Order

When both y and en appear: **y** before **en**

| Order | Example |
|-------|---------|
| y + en | *Il y en a trois* (There are three) |
| me + y | *Tu m'y emmènes?* |
| me + en | *Tu m'en donnes?* |
| y + en (both) | *J'y en mets* (I put some there) — less common |

💡 **Tip**: *Il y en a* is extremely common. Practice it!

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **j'en ai marre** — *I'm fed up*
- **il faut y aller** — *we have to go*
- **s'en aller** — *to leave*
- **en avoir assez** — *to have enough (of something)*
- **y penser** — *to think about it*

quizPairs.question: J'y vais. J'en reviens
quizPairs.answer: I'm going there. I'm coming back from there

quizPairs.question: Tu m'en donnes ? / Tu m'y emmènes ?
quizPairs.answer: Can you give me some? / Can you take me there?

quizPairs.question: On en parle ? — Oui, j'y pense
quizPairs.answer: Shall we talk about it? — Yes, I'm thinking about it

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 14: The Relative Pronoun "dont"

Welcome back! **Dont** is a relative pronoun meaning "of which", "whose", or "about which". It replaces *de* + noun in relative clauses. It connects a main clause to extra information.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### C'est le livre dont je t'ai parlé
> *It's the book I told you about*
> *Dont* replaces *de ce livre* in *je t'ai parlé de ce livre*. Pronounced: say luh leevr dohn zhuh tay par-lay.

### La femme dont le fils est médecin
> *The woman whose son is a doctor*
> *Dont* = whose. *Dont* + noun + verb.

### Voici la ville dont je viens
> *Here's the town I come from*
> *Dont* replaces *de laquelle* (from which).

### C'est quelque chose dont je suis fier
> *It's something I'm proud of*
> *Être fier de* → *dont je suis fier*.

### Les amis dont je me souviens
> *The friends I remember*
> *Se souvenir de* → *dont je me souviens*.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: When to Use "dont"

| Verb/Expression | With de | Relative with dont |
|-----------------|---------|---------------------|
| parler de | je parle de ce livre | le livre dont je parle |
| avoir besoin de | j'ai besoin de ça | ce dont j'ai besoin |
| être fier de | je suis fier de ça | ce dont je suis fier |
| se souvenir de | je me souviens de lui | la personne dont je me souviens |

💡 **Tip**: If the verb or expression uses *de*, use *dont* in the relative clause.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **ce dont** — *what/that which* (*Ce dont j'ai besoin* = What I need)
- **dont j'ai besoin** — *that I need*
- **dont je suis content** — *that I'm happy about*
- **la raison dont** — *the reason why* (less common)
- **au sujet de** → *dont* — *about which*

quizPairs.question: C'est le livre dont je t'ai parlé
quizPairs.answer: It's the book I told you about

quizPairs.question: La femme dont le fils est médecin
quizPairs.answer: The woman whose son is a doctor

quizPairs.question: Voici la ville dont je viens
quizPairs.answer: Here's the town I come from

quizPairs.question: C'est quelque chose dont je suis fier
Les amis dont je me souviens

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 15: Review — y, en, dont

Welcome back! This review consolidates the pronouns **y**, **en**, and **dont**. You'll practice replacing places, quantities, and "of which" in real conversations.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases (Recap)

### Tu y vas ? — Oui, j'y vais
> *Are you going there? — Yes, I'm going there*
> Y = there (à + place).

### Combien en as-tu ? — J'en ai trois
> *How many do you have? — I have three*
> En = of them (quantity).

### Il y en a assez
> *There's enough (of them)*
> Il y a + en.

### C'est ce dont j'ai besoin
> *That's what I need*
> Dont = of which / that which.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Quick Reference

| Pronoun | Replaces | Example |
|---------|----------|---------|
| y | à + place/thing | J'y vais |
| en | de + thing/quantity | J'en ai deux |
| dont | de in relative clause | le livre dont je parle |

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **avoir besoin de** — *to need*
- **se souvenir de** — *to remember*
- **parler de** — *to talk about*
- **penser à** — *to think about* (→ y)
- **ce dont** — *what/that which*

quizPairs.question: Tu y vas ? — Oui, j'y vais
quizPairs.answer: Are you going there? — Yes, I'm going there

quizPairs.question: Combien en as-tu ? — J'en ai trois
quizPairs.answer: How many do you have? — I have three

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 16: Future Simple — Formation (Regular Verbs)

Welcome back! You know the **futur proche** (je vais faire). Now meet the **futur simple** — the "real" future tense for more formal or distant future. Formation: infinitive + endings -ai, -as, -a, -ons, -ez, -ont.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je parlerai français
> *I will speak French*
> Parler + ai = parlerai. Pronounced: zhuh par-luh-ray frahn-say.

### Tu mangeras à la maison
> *You will eat at home*
> Manger + as = mangeras. Pronounced: too mahn-zhuh-rah ah lah may-zohn.

### Il viendra demain
> *He will come tomorrow*
> Regular -re: vendre → il vendra. Venir is irregular — we'll see next lesson!

### Nous finirons bientôt
> *We will finish soon*
> Finir + ons = finirons. Pronounced: noo fee-nee-rohn byan-toh.

### Vous partirez quand ?
> *When will you leave?*
> Partir + ez = partirez.

### Elles habiteront à Paris
> *They will live in Paris*
> Habiter + ont = habiteront. Pronounced: ell zah-bee-tuh-rohn ah pah-ree.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Future Simple Formation

Take the **infinitive** and add: -ai, -as, -a, -ons, -ez, -ont

| -er | -ir | -re |
|-----|-----|-----|
| je parlerai | je finirai | je vendrai |
| tu parleras | tu finiras | tu vendras |
| il parlera | il finira | il vendra |
| nous parlerons | nous finirons | nous vendrons |
| vous parlerez | vous finirez | vous vendrez |
| ils parleront | ils finiront | ils vendront |

💡 **Note**: -er verbs drop the final -e before adding endings. Parler → je parlerai (not parlerei).

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **demain** — *tomorrow*
- **l'année prochaine** — *next year*
- **bientôt** — *soon*
- **plus tard** — *later*
- **un jour** — *one day / someday*

## Lesson 17: Future Simple — Irregular Stems

Welcome back! Some common verbs have irregular stems in the future simple. The endings stay the same (-ai, -as, -a, etc.), but the stem changes. Here are the most important ones.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### J'irai à Paris
> *I will go to Paris*
> Aller → ir- + ending. Pronounced: zhee-ray ah pah-ree.

### Tu auras le temps
> *You will have time*
> Avoir → aur- + ending. Pronounced: too oh-ra luh tahn.

### Il sera content
> *He will be happy*
> Être → ser- + ending. Pronounced: eel suh-rah kohn-tahn.

### Nous ferons une fête
> *We will have a party*
> Faire → fer- + ending. Pronounced: noo fuh-rohn oon fet.

### Vous viendrez ?
> *Will you come?*
> Venir → viendr- + ending. Pronounced: voo vyan-dray.

### Ils pourront nous aider
> *They will be able to help us*
> Pouvoir → pourr- + ending. Pronounced: eel poo-rohn noo zay-day.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Key Irregular Stems

| Infinitive | Future stem | Example |
|------------|-------------|---------|
| aller | ir- | j'irai |
| avoir | aur- | j'aurai |
| être | ser- | je serai |
| faire | fer- | je ferai |
| venir | viendr- | je viendrai |
| pouvoir | pourr- | je pourrai |
| vouloir | voudr- | je voudrai |
| savoir | saur- | je saurai |

💡 **Tip**: Learn these stems — they're used in the conditional too!

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **je pourrai** — *I will be able to*
- **je voudrai** — *I will want to*
- **je saurai** — *I will know*
- **il faudra** — *it will be necessary*
- **il y aura** — *there will be*

quizPairs.question: Ils pourront nous aider
quizPairs.answer: They will be able to help us

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 18: Future vs Futur Proche — When to Use Each

Welcome back! You now know both **futur simple** and **futur proche** (aller + infinitive). When do we use which? Here's the practical guide.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je vais partir demain (futur proche)
> *I'm going to leave tomorrow*
> Near future, already planned. More casual.

### Je partirai l'année prochaine (futur simple)
> *I will leave next year*
> Distant or uncertain future. More formal.

### On va manger dans une heure
> *We're going to eat in an hour*
> Futur proche: soon, concrete plan.

### Un jour, je visiterai le Japon
> *One day, I will visit Japan*
> Futur simple: dream, distant, uncertain.

### Il va pleuvoir
> *It's going to rain*
> Weather predictions often use futur proche (near).

### Les prix augmenteront
> *Prices will increase*
> General prediction, formal context — futur simple.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Futur Proche vs Futur Simple

| Futur proche (aller + inf) | Futur simple |
|---------------------------|--------------|
| Near future (hours, days) | Distant future (months, years) |
| Concrete, planned | Hypothetical, formal |
| Spoken, casual | Written, formal |
| *Je vais faire* | *Je ferai* |

💡 **Tip**: In spoken French, futur proche is more common. Use futur simple for distant plans or formal writing.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **dans + time** — *in* (dans une heure = in an hour)
- **bientôt** — *soon*
- **plus tard** — *later*
- **un jour** — *someday*
- **probablement** — *probably*

quizPairs.question: On va manger dans une heure
quizPairs.answer: We're going to eat in an hour

quizPairs.question: Un jour, je visiterai le Japon
quizPairs.answer: One day, I will visit Japan

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 19: Comparatives & Superlatives

Welcome back! Today you'll learn to compare things in French: "more than", "less than", "as... as", and "the most". Essential for opinions and descriptions.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### C'est plus grand que...
> *It's bigger than...*
> Plus + adjective + que. Pronounced: say ploo grahn kuh.

### Elle est moins chère que celle-ci
> *It's less expensive than this one*
> Moins + adjective + que.

### Tu es aussi rapide que lui
> *You're as fast as him*
> Aussi + adjective + que. Pronounced: too ay oh-see rah-peed kuh lwee.

### C'est le plus beau
> *It's the most beautiful*
> Le/la/les + plus + adjective. Pronounced: say luh ploo boh.

### C'est la moins chère
> *It's the cheapest*
> Le/la/les + moins + adjective.

### Il court le plus vite
> *He runs the fastest*
> Le plus + adverb (vite, bien, mal).

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Comparison Formulas

| Type | Formula | Example |
|------|---------|---------|
| More than | plus + adj + que | plus grand que |
| Less than | moins + adj + que | moins cher que |
| As... as | aussi + adj + que | aussi bon que |
| Most | le/la/les + plus + adj | le plus grand |
| Least | le/la/les + moins + adj | le moins cher |

💡 **Tip**: *Que* = than. *Bon* → *meilleur* (better), *bien* → *mieux* (better) — irregular!

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **meilleur(e)** — *better* (adj)
- **mieux** — *better* (adv)
- **pire** — *worse*
- **de plus en plus** — *more and more*
- **de moins en moins** — *less and less*

quizPairs.question: Elle est moins chère que celle-ci
quizPairs.answer: It's less expensive than this one

quizPairs.question: Tu es aussi rapide que lui

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 20: Review — Future & Comparisons

Welcome back! This review brings together the Future Simple (regular and irregular), futur proche vs futur simple, and comparatives/superlatives. Let's practice it all.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases (Recap)

### Je viendrai / Je vais venir
> *I will come / I'm going to come*
> Both correct — choose by near vs distant.

### C'est le plus difficile
> *It's the most difficult*
> Superlative with le plus.

### Elle est moins chère que l'autre
> *It's cheaper than the other one*
> Comparative with moins que.

### Un jour, j'irai en France
> *One day, I'll go to France*
> Futur simple for distant dream.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Quick Reference

- **Futur simple**: infinitive + -ai/-as/-a/-ons/-ez/-ont (irregular stems for aller, avoir, être...)
- **Futur proche**: aller + infinitive (near future)
- **Comparatives**: plus/moins/aussi + adj + que
- **Superlatives**: le/la/les + plus/moins + adj

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **meilleur** — *better* (irregular)
- **mieux** — *better* (adverb)
- **probablement** — *probably*
- **bientôt** — *soon*

quizPairs.question: Je viendrai / Je vais venir
quizPairs.answer: I will come / I'm going to come

quizPairs.question: Elle est moins chère que l'autre
quizPairs.answer: It's cheaper than the other one

quizPairs.question: Un jour, j'irai en France
quizPairs.answer: One day, I'll go to France

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 21: Airports & Flying

Welcome back! Today you'll learn essential vocabulary for airports and flying in French — booking flights, checking in, security, and boarding.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je voudrais réserver un vol
> *I would like to book a flight*
> Pronounced: zhuh voo-dreh ray-zehr-ver uhN vol. Essential for travel.

### Où est l'enregistrement ?
> *Where is check-in?*
> Pronounced: oo ay lahn-reh-zhee-struh-mahn. *Enregistrement* = check-in.

### Le vol est à l'heure / en retard
> *The flight is on time / delayed*
> Pronounced: luh vol ay ah lur / ahn ruh-tar.

### Passeport et carte d'embarquement, s'il vous plaît
> *Passport and boarding pass, please*
> Pronounced: pas-por ay kart ahn-bar-kuh-mahn seel voo pleh. At security/gate.

### Où est la douane ?
> *Where is customs?*
> Pronounced: oo ay lah doo-ahn. After arrival.

### Bagage à main / bagage en soute
> *Hand luggage / checked baggage*
> Pronounced: bah-gazh ah man / bah-gazh ahn soot.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Airport Vocabulary

| French | English |
|--------|---------|
| un vol | a flight |
| l'enregistrement | check-in |
| la porte d'embarquement | boarding gate |
| la carte d'embarquement | boarding pass |
| la douane | customs |
| le tapis roulant | baggage carousel |

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **un aller simple / aller-retour** — *one-way / return ticket*
- **s'embarquer** — *to board*
- **décoller / atterrir** — *to take off / to land*
- **la zone de transit** — *transit area*
- **faire la queue** — *to queue / stand in line*

quizPairs.question: Je voudrais réserver un vol
quizPairs.answer: I would like to book a flight

quizPairs.question: Le vol est à l'heure / en retard
quizPairs.answer: The flight is on time / delayed

quizPairs.question: Passeport et carte d'embarquement, s'il vous plaît

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 22: Train Travel

Welcome back! Trains are a great way to travel in France. Today you'll learn vocabulary for the station, tickets, platforms, and connections.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Un billet pour Lyon, s'il vous plaît
> *A ticket to Lyon, please*
> Pronounced: uhN bee-yay poor lee-ohn seel voo pleh.

### Où est le quai numéro 5 ?
> *Where is platform 5?*
> Pronounced: oo ay luh kay noo-may-ro sank. *Quai* = platform.

### Y a-t-il une correspondance ?
> *Is there a connection?*
> Pronounced: yah-teel oon ko-reh-spohn-dahns. Changing trains.

### Le train part à quelle heure ?
> *What time does the train leave?*
> Pronounced: luh trahn par ah kel ur.

### Je voudrais un aller-retour
> *I would like a return ticket*
> Pronounced: zhuh voo-dreh uhN ah-lay ruh-toor.

### Le train est en retard
> *The train is delayed*
> Same structure as flights: *en retard*.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Train Vocabulary

| French | English |
|--------|---------|
| la gare | the station |
| le quai | the platform |
| le billet | the ticket |
| la correspondance | the connection |
| le TGV | high-speed train |
| le guichet | the ticket office |

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **composter son billet** — *to validate your ticket* (before boarding)
- **la voie** — *the track*
- **un train direct** — *a direct train*
- **changer de train** — *to change trains*
- **à l'heure / en avance** — *on time / early*

quizPairs.question: Un billet pour Lyon, s'il vous plaît
Le train part à quelle heure ?
quizPairs.answer: What time does the train leave?

quizPairs.question: Je voudrais un aller-retour
quizPairs.answer: I would like a return ticket

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 23: Renting & Accommodation

Welcome back! Today you'll learn vocabulary for renting an apartment or house in France — agencies, contracts, deposits, and utilities.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je cherche un appartement à louer
> *I'm looking for an apartment to rent*
> Pronounced: zhuh shairsh uhN ah-par-tuh-mahn ah loo-ay. *Louer* = to rent.

### Quel est le loyer ?
> *What is the rent?*
> Pronounced: kel ay luh lwah-yay. *Loyer* = rent.

### Les charges sont incluses ?
> *Are utilities included?*
> Pronounced: lay sharsh sohn-tan-kloo-z. *Charges* = utilities/fees.

### Il faut signer le contrat
> *You have to sign the contract*
> Pronounced: eel foh see-nyay luh kohn-trah. *Il faut* = it's necessary.

### Je voudrais visiter l'appartement
> *I would like to visit the apartment*
> Pronounced: zhuh voo-dreh vee-zee-tay lah-par-tuh-mahn.

### C'est meublé ou non meublé ?
> *Is it furnished or unfurnished?*
> Pronounced: say muh-blay oo nohn muh-blay.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Renting Vocabulary

| French | English |
|--------|---------|
| louer | to rent |
| le loyer | the rent |
| la caution / le dépôt | the deposit |
| le contrat | the contract |
| les charges | utilities |
| l'agence immobilière | the estate agency |

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **un bail** — *a lease*
- **un état des lieux** — *inventory / inspection*
- **la clé** — *the key*
- **rendre les clés** — *to hand back the keys*
- **un préavis** — *notice period*

quizPairs.question: Je cherche un appartement à louer
quizPairs.answer: I'm looking for an apartment to rent

quizPairs.question: Il faut signer le contrat
quizPairs.answer: You have to sign the contract

quizPairs.question: Je voudrais visiter l'appartement
quizPairs.answer: I would like to visit the apartment

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 24: Media & Technology

Welcome back! Today you'll learn vocabulary for media and technology — TV, podcasts, social media, and common tech terms in French.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je regarde la télé
> *I watch TV*
> Pronounced: zhuh ruh-gard lah tay-lay. *La télé* = television.

### J'écoute un podcast
> *I listen to a podcast*
> Pronounced: zhay-koot uhN pod-cast. Same word in French!

### Tu es sur les réseaux sociaux ?
> *Are you on social media?*
> Pronounced: too ay soor lay ray-zoh soh-syoh. *Réseaux sociaux* = social media.

### Je vais envoyer un message
> *I'm going to send a message*
> Pronounced: zhuh vay ahn-vwah-yay uhN may-sazh.

### Le Wi-Fi ne marche pas
> *The Wi-Fi doesn't work*
> Pronounced: luh wee-fee nuh marsh pah. *Marcher* = to work (for devices).

### Je te tague sur la photo
> *I'll tag you in the photo*
> Pronounced: zhuh tuh tahg soor lah foh-toh. *Tagger* = to tag (anglicism).

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Media Vocabulary

| French | English |
|--------|---------|
| la télé / la télévision | TV |
| un podcast | a podcast |
| les réseaux sociaux | social media |
| une application / une appli | an app |
| un message | a message |
| naviguer sur Internet | to browse the Internet |

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **télécharger** — *to download*
- **envoyer / recevoir** — *to send / receive*
- **une notification** — *a notification*
- **mettre à jour** — *to update*
- **hors ligne** — *offline*

quizPairs.question: Tu es sur les réseaux sociaux ?
Je vais envoyer un message
quizPairs.answer: I'm going to send a message

## 🔤 Key Phrases (Recap)

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 26: Subjunctive Introduction — il faut que, vouloir que

Welcome back! The **subjonctif** (subjunctive) is a mood used after certain expressions: wishes, necessity, doubt. It often appears after *il faut que* and *vouloir que*. Formation: stem (3rd person plural present) + -e, -es, -e, -ions, -iez, -ent.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Il faut que je parte
> *I have to leave*
> *Il faut que* + subjunctive. Pronounced: eel foh kuh zhuh part. *Partir* → que je parte.

### Je veux que tu viennes
> *I want you to come*
> *Vouloir que* + subjunctive. Pronounced: zhuh vuh kuh too vyehn.

### Il faut qu'on y aille
> *We have to go*
> *Aller* → qu'on aille (irregular subjunctive). Pronounced: eel foh kohn ee ahy.

### C'est important que vous soyez à l'heure
> *It's important that you be on time*
> *Être* → que vous soyez. Pronounced: say tan-por-tahn kuh voo swah-yay ah lur.

### Je préfère qu'il reste
> *I prefer that he stay*
> *Préférer que* + subjunctive. *Rester* → qu'il reste.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Subjunctive Formation (Regular -er)

Take **ils** form, drop **-ent**, add: -e, -es, -e, -ions, -iez, -ent

| parler (ils parlent) | que je parle | que tu parles | qu'il parle | que nous parlions | que vous parliez | qu'ils parlent |
|----------------------|--------------|---------------|-------------|-------------------|------------------|----------------|

**Irregular**: être → sois, sois, soit, soyons, soyez, soient | avoir → aie, aies, ait, ayons, ayez, aient | aller → aille, ailles, aille, allions, alliez, aillent

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il faut que** — *it's necessary that*
- **vouloir que** — *to want that*
- **préférer que** — *to prefer that*
- **il est important que** — *it's important that*
- **pour que** — *so that*

quizPairs.question: C'est important que vous soyez à l'heure
quizPairs.answer: It's important that you be on time

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 27: Subjunctive — Wishes & Emotions

Welcome back! The subjunctive is used after expressions of emotion and wish: *être content que*, *être triste que*, *vouloir que*, *souhaiter que*. Let's practice these.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je suis content(e) que tu sois là
> *I'm glad you're here*
> *Être content que* + subjunctive. *Être* → que tu soies. Pronounced: zhuh swee kohn-tahn kuh too swah lah.

### C'est dommage qu'il ne vienne pas
> *It's a shame he's not coming*
> *Dommage que* + subjunctive. Pronounced: say doh-mazh keel nuh vyehn pah.

### Je souhaite que tout aille bien
> *I hope everything goes well*
> *Souhaiter que* + subjunctive. *Aller* → que tout aille.

### Elle est triste que nous partions
> *She's sad that we're leaving*
> *Être triste que* + subjunctive. *Partir* → que nous partions.

### Il est possible que ce soit fermé
> *It's possible it's closed*
> *Il est possible que* + subjunctive. *Être* → que ce soit.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Subjunctive After Emotion/Wish

| Expression | Example |
|------------|---------|
| être content que | Je suis content qu'il vienne |
| c'est dommage que | C'est dommage qu'elle parte |
| souhaiter que | Je souhaite que tu réussisses |
| être triste que | Je suis triste qu'il pleuve |
| il est possible que | Il est possible que ce soit vrai |

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **être ravi(e) que** — *to be delighted that*
- **avoir peur que** — *to be afraid that*
- **espérer que** — *to hope that* (takes indicative, not subjunctive!)
- **regretter que** — *to regret that*
- **être surpris(e) que** — *to be surprised that*

quizPairs.question: Je suis content(e) que tu sois là
C'est dommage qu'il ne vienne pas
quizPairs.answer: It's a shame he's not coming

quizPairs.question: Je souhaite que tout aille bien
Elle est triste que nous partions
Il est possible que ce soit fermé

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 28: Subjunctive — Doubt & Necessity

Welcome back! The subjunctive also follows expressions of doubt, denial, and necessity: *je ne crois pas que*, *il est nécessaire que*, *il est possible que*. Let's master these.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je ne crois pas qu'il vienne
> *I don't think he's coming*
> *Croire* (negative) + *que* + subjunctive. Pronounced: zhuh nuh krwah pah keel vyehn.

### Il est nécessaire que nous partions tôt
> *It's necessary that we leave early*
> *Il est nécessaire que* + subjunctive. Pronounced: eel ay nay-say-sair kuh noo par-tyohn toh.

### Il est possible que ce soit vrai
> *It's possible it's true*
> *Il est possible que* + subjunctive. *Être* → que ce soit.

### Je doute qu'elle le sache
> *I doubt she knows*
> *Douter que* + subjunctive. *Savoir* → qu'elle sache (irregular).

### Il n'est pas certain qu'il pleuve
> *It's not certain it will rain*
> *Il n'est pas certain que* + subjunctive.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Subjunctive After Doubt/Necessity

| Affirmative (indicative) | Negative/Doubt (subjunctive) |
|-------------------------|------------------------------|
| Je crois qu'il vient | Je ne crois pas qu'il vienne |
| Je pense que c'est vrai | Je ne pense pas que ce soit vrai |
| Il est certain qu'il viendra | Il n'est pas certain qu'il vienne |

**Necessity**: il faut que, il est nécessaire que, il est important que → always subjunctive

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **douter que** — *to doubt that*
- **il est possible que** — *it's possible that*
- **il est nécessaire que** — *it's necessary that*
- **il n'est pas sûr que** — *it's not sure that*
- **savoir** (subj.) — *sache, saches, sache, sachions, sachiez, sachent*

quizPairs.question: Il est nécessaire que nous partions tôt
quizPairs.answer: It's necessary that we leave early

quizPairs.question: Il est possible que ce soit vrai
Il n'est pas certain qu'il pleuve
quizPairs.answer: It's not certain it will rain

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 29: Demonstratives & Possessives

Welcome back! Today you'll learn **demonstrative adjectives** (ce, cet, cette, ces — this/that/these/those) and **possessive pronouns** (le mien, le tien — mine, yours). Essential for pointing and ownership.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ce livre / cette maison
> *This book / this house*
> *Ce* (masc), *cette* (fem). *Cet* before vowel: *cet appartement*. Pronounced: suh leevr / set may-zohn.

### Ces chaussures sont belles
> *These shoes are beautiful*
> *Ces* = these/those (plural). Pronounced: say shoh-sir sohn bell.

### C'est le mien / C'est la mienne
> *It's mine*
> Possessive pronoun agrees with noun: *le mien* (masc), *la mienne* (fem). Pronounced: say luh myan / say lah myehn.

### C'est le tien ou le sien ?
> *Is it yours or his/hers?*
> *Le tien* = yours (masc). *Le sien* = his/hers (masc). Pronounced: say luh tyan oo luh syan.

### Voici mon portable. Où est le vôtre ?
> *Here's my phone. Where is yours?*
> *Le vôtre* = yours (formal/plural). *Le nôtre* = ours.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Demonstrative & Possessive

| Demonstrative | Masc | Fem | Plural |
|---------------|------|-----|--------|
| This/that | ce, cet (vowel) | cette | ces |
| Mine | le mien | la mienne | les miens/miennes |
| Yours | le tien | la tienne | les tiens/tiennes |
| His/hers | le sien | la sienne | les siens/siennes |
| Ours | le nôtre | la nôtre | les nôtres |
| Yours (formal) | le vôtre | la vôtre | les vôtres |
| Theirs | le leur | la leur | les leurs |

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **celui-ci / celle-ci** — *this one* (masc/fem)
- **celui-là / celle-là** — *that one*
- **à moi / à toi** — *mine / yours* (stress form)
- **c'est à moi** — *it's mine*

quizPairs.question: C'est le mien / C'est la mienne

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 30: Review — Subjunctive & Demonstratives

Welcome back! This review consolidates the subjunctive (il faut que, vouloir que, emotion, doubt) and demonstrative/possessive pronouns. Let's practice it all.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases (Recap)

### Il faut que je parte. Je veux que tu viennes
> *I have to leave. I want you to come*
> Subjunctive after necessity and wish.

### Je suis content qu'il soit là. Je ne crois pas qu'il vienne
> *I'm glad he's here. I don't think he's coming*
> Subjunctive after emotion and doubt.

### Ce livre est le mien. Ces chaussures sont les tiennes
> *This book is mine. These shoes are yours*
> Demonstrative + possessive pronoun.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Quick Reference

- **Subjunctive**: after il faut que, vouloir que, être content que, je ne crois pas que
- **Demonstratives**: ce, cet, cette, ces
- **Possessives**: le mien, la mienne, le tien, le sien, le nôtre, le vôtre, le leur

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il est nécessaire que** — *it's necessary that*
- **c'est dommage que** — *it's a shame that*
- **celui-ci / celui-là** — *this one / that one*

quizPairs.question: Il faut que je parte. Je veux que tu viennes
quizPairs.answer: I have to leave. I want you to come

quizPairs.question: Je suis content qu'il soit là. Je ne crois pas qu'il vienne
quizPairs.answer: I'm glad he's here. I don't think he's coming

quizPairs.question: Ce livre est le mien. Ces chaussures sont les tiennes
quizPairs.answer: This book is mine. These shoes are yours

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 31: Work & Careers

Welcome back! Today you'll learn vocabulary for job hunting, interviews, CVs, and the workplace — essential for professional French.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je cherche un emploi
> *I'm looking for a job*
> Pronounced: zhuh shairsh uhN ahn-plwah. *Emploi* = job.

### J'ai un entretien demain
> *I have an interview tomorrow*
> Pronounced: zhay uhN ahn-truh-tyan duh-man. *Entretien* = interview.

### Voici mon CV
> *Here's my CV / resume*
> Pronounced: vwah-see mohn say-vay. *CV* = curriculum vitae (same as English).

### Quelle est votre expérience ?
> *What is your experience?*
> Pronounced: kel ay vohtr ek-speh-ryahns. Common interview question.

### Je travaille dans une entreprise
> *I work for a company*
> Pronounced: zhuh trah-vahy dahn oon ahn-truh-preez. *Entreprise* = company.

### Le salaire est négociable
> *The salary is negotiable*
> Pronounced: luh sah-lair ay nay-goh-syah-bluh.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Work Vocabulary

| French | English |
|--------|---------|
| chercher un emploi | to look for a job |
| un entretien | an interview |
| un CV | a CV / resume |
| une entreprise | a company |
| le salaire | the salary |
| un collègue | a colleague |

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **postuler** — *to apply*
- **embaucher** — *to hire*
- **licencier** — *to lay off*
- **être à temps plein / partiel** — *to work full-time / part-time*
- **un stage** — *an internship*

## Lesson 32: Education & Studies

Welcome back! Today you'll learn vocabulary for education — university, courses, exams, and diplomas.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je fais des études à l'université
> *I'm studying at the university*
> Pronounced: zhuh fay day zay-too-d ay loo-nee-vair-see-tay. *Faire des études* = to study.

### J'ai un cours à neuf heures
> *I have a class at nine*
> Pronounced: zhay uhN koor ah nuhv ur. *Cours* = class/course.

### L'examen est la semaine prochaine
> *The exam is next week*
> Pronounced: lay-gzah-men ay lah suh-men pro-shen. *Examen* = exam.

### J'ai obtenu mon diplôme
> *I got my diploma*
> Pronounced: zhay ohb-tuh-noo mohn dee-plohm. *Obtenir* = to obtain/get.

### Où est l'amphi ?
> *Where is the lecture hall?*
> Pronounced: oo ay lahm-fee. *Amphi* = short for *amphithéâtre*.

### Je dois rendre un mémoire
> *I have to hand in a thesis*
> Pronounced: zhuh dwah rahn-dr uhN may-mwar. *Mémoire* = thesis/dissertation.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Education Vocabulary

| French | English |
|--------|---------|
| l'université | the university |
| un cours | a class/course |
| un examen | an exam |
| un diplôme | a diploma |
| la licence | bachelor's degree |
| le master | master's degree |

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **être en licence / en master** — *to be in undergrad / grad school*
- **passer un examen** — *to take an exam*
- **réussir / rater** — *to pass / to fail*
- **la bibliothèque** — *the library*
- **un professeur / un prof** — *a professor / teacher*

quizPairs.question: Je fais des études à l'université
J'ai un cours à neuf heures
quizPairs.answer: I have a class at nine

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 33: Health & Pharmacy

Welcome back! Today you'll learn vocabulary for the pharmacy, prescriptions, and common symptoms — essential for health situations in French-speaking countries.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je vais à la pharmacie
> *I'm going to the pharmacy*
> Pronounced: zhuh vay ah lah far-mah-see. *Pharmacie* = pharmacy.

### J'ai besoin d'une ordonnance
> *I need a prescription*
> Pronounced: zhay buh-zwan doon or-doh-nahns. *Ordonnance* = prescription.

### J'ai mal à la tête
> *I have a headache*
> Pronounced: zhay mahl ah lah tet. *Avoir mal à* = to have pain in.

### J'ai de la fièvre
> *I have a fever*
> Pronounced: zhay duh lah fyehvruh. *Fièvre* = fever.

### Avez-vous quelque chose pour la toux ?
> *Do you have something for the cough?*
> Pronounced: ah-vay voo kel-kuh shohz poor lah too. *Toux* = cough.

### Prenez deux comprimés par jour
> *Take two tablets per day*
> Pronounced: pruh-nay duh kohm-pree-may par zhoor. *Comprimé* = tablet.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Health Vocabulary

| French | English |
|--------|---------|
| la pharmacie | the pharmacy |
| une ordonnance | a prescription |
| avoir mal à | to have pain in |
| la fièvre | fever |
| la toux | cough |
| un comprimé | a tablet |

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **les symptômes** — *symptoms*
- **un médicament** — *medicine*
- **sans ordonnance** — *over the counter*
- **la posologie** — *dosage*
- **consultation** — *consultation*

quizPairs.question: Avez-vous quelque chose pour la toux ?
quizPairs.answer: Do you have something for the cough?

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 34: French Culture & Cinema

Welcome back! Today you'll explore French culture and cinema — film vocabulary, cultural expressions, and talking about French movies.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### J'adore le cinéma français
> *I love French cinema*
> Pronounced: zhah-dor luh see-nay-mah frahn-seh. *Cinéma* = cinema.

### C'est un film de Truffaut
> *It's a film by Truffaut*
> Pronounced: say tuhN feelm duh troo-foh. *Film* is the same in French.

### On va voir un film ce soir ?
> *Shall we see a film tonight?*
> Pronounced: ohn vah vwar uhN feelm suh swar. *Voir un film* = to see a movie.

### C'est un chef-d'œuvre
> *It's a masterpiece*
> Pronounced: say tuhN sheh-duh-vruh. *Chef-d'œuvre* = masterpiece.

### La séance est à quelle heure ?
> *What time is the showing?*
> Pronounced: lah say-ahns ay ah kel ur. *Séance* = screening/showing.

### Ce film m'a beaucoup plu
> *I really liked this film*
> Pronounced: suh feelm mah boh-koo ploo. *Plaire* (to please) in passé composé.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Cinema Vocabulary

| French | English |
|--------|---------|
| le cinéma | the cinema |
| un film | a film |
| la séance | the showing |
| le réalisateur | the director |
| jouer dans un film | to act in a film |
| les sous-titres | subtitles |

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **un long métrage** — *a feature film*
- **un court métrage** — *a short film*
- **la bande-annonce** — *the trailer*
- **C'est typiquement français** — *It's typically French*
- **la gastronomie** — *gastronomy / cuisine*

quizPairs.question: On va voir un film ce soir ?
quizPairs.answer: Shall we see a film tonight?

quizPairs.question: La séance est à quelle heure ?

## 🔦 Spotlight: Quick Reference

| Health | pharmacie, ordonnance, mal à la tête |

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 36: Advanced Negation

Welcome back! You know *ne...pas*. Now let's expand: *ne...rien* (nothing), *ne...personne* (nobody), *ne...jamais* (never), and *ne...ni...ni* (neither...nor). These make your French much more precise.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je n'ai rien mangé
> *I didn't eat anything*
> *Ne...rien* = nothing. Pronounced: zhuh nay ryan mahn-zhay. *Rien* comes after the verb in compound tenses.

### Il n'y a personne
> *There's nobody*
> *Ne...personne* = nobody/no one. Pronounced: eel nee ah pair-sohn.

### Je ne mange jamais de viande
> *I never eat meat*
> *Ne...jamais* = never. Pronounced: zhuh nuh mahnzh zhah-may duh vee-ahnd.

### Je n'ai ni frère ni sœur
> *I have neither brother nor sister*
> *Ne...ni...ni* = neither...nor. Pronounced: zhuh nay nee frair nee sur.

### Il ne dit plus rien
> *He doesn't say anything anymore*
> *Ne...plus...rien* = no longer anything. *Plus* = no more.

### On n'a jamais vu ça
> *We've never seen that*
> *Ne...jamais* with passé composé. Pronounced: ohn nah zhah-may vyoo sah.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Negation Formulas

| Negation | Meaning | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| ne...pas | not | Je ne sais pas |
| ne...rien | nothing | Je n'ai rien dit |
| ne...personne | nobody | Je ne vois personne |
| ne...jamais | never | Je ne mange jamais |
| ne...plus | no more | Je n'ai plus faim |
| ne...ni...ni | neither...nor | Je n'ai ni temps ni argent |

💡 **Tip**: In compound tenses, *rien* and *personne* go after the past participle: *J'ai rien mangé* (informal), *Je n'ai rien mangé* (formal).

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **ne...aucun(e)** — *none*
- **ne...nulle part** — *nowhere*
- **ne...guère** — *hardly*
- **plus rien** — *nothing anymore*
- **jamais rien** — *never anything*

quizPairs.question: Je n'ai ni frère ni sœur
quizPairs.answer: I have neither brother nor sister

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 37: Connaître vs Savoir

Welcome back! Both *connaître* and *savoir* mean "to know" in English, but they're used differently. *Connaître* = to know (a person, place, thing). *Savoir* = to know (a fact, how to do something).

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Je connais Paris
> *I know Paris* (I've been there, I'm familiar with it)
> *Connaître* + noun (person, place). Pronounced: zhuh koh-nay pah-ree.

### Je sais qu'il vient
> *I know (that) he's coming*
> *Savoir* + clause or fact. Pronounced: zhuh say keel vyan.

### Tu connais Marie ?
> *Do you know Marie?*
> *Connaître* for people. Pronounced: too koh-nay mah-ree.

### Je sais nager
> *I know how to swim*
> *Savoir* + infinitive = to know how to. Pronounced: zhuh say nah-zhay.

### Il ne sait pas conduire
> *He doesn't know how to drive*
> *Savoir* for skills. Pronounced: eel nuh say pah kohn-dweer.

### Nous connaissons bien ce quartier
> *We know this neighbourhood well*
> *Connaître* for places. Pronounced: noo koh-nay-sohn byan suh kar-tyay.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Connaître vs Savoir

| Connaître | Savoir |
|-----------|--------|
| People (Je connais Paul) | Facts (Je sais qu'il pleut) |
| Places (Je connais Lyon) | How to (Je sais cuisiner) |
| Things (Je connais ce film) | Information (Je ne sais pas) |

**Connaître** = familiarity. **Savoir** = knowledge/skill.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **savoir-faire** — *know-how*
- **faire la connaissance de** — *to meet* (make the acquaintance of)
- **à savoir** — *namely / that is*
- **Je ne sais pas** — *I don't know*
- **pour autant que je sache** — *as far as I know*

quizPairs.question: Il ne sait pas conduire
quizPairs.answer: He doesn't know how to drive

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 38: Depuis, Pendant, Pour — Prepositions of Duration

Welcome back! *Depuis*, *pendant*, and *pour* all relate to duration, but they're used differently. *Depuis* = since/for (ongoing). *Pendant* = during/for (completed). *Pour* = for (planned).

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### J'habite ici depuis cinq ans
> *I've been living here for five years*
> *Depuis* + duration = ongoing. Use present tense! Pronounced: zhah-beet ee-see duh-pwee sank ahn.

### J'ai travaillé pendant deux heures
> *I worked for two hours*
> *Pendant* + duration = during (completed period). Pronounced: zhay trah-vahy-yay pahn-dahn duh zur.

### Je pars pour une semaine
> *I'm leaving for a week*
> *Pour* + duration = for (planned). Pronounced: zhuh par poor oon suh-men.

### Depuis quand tu le connais ?
> *Since when have you known him?*
> *Depuis quand* = since when. Pronounced: duh-pwee kahn too luh koh-nay.

### Il a plu pendant la nuit
> *It rained during the night*
> *Pendant* + noun = during. Pronounced: eel ah ploo pahn-dahn lah nwee.

### Je vais rester pour trois jours
> *I'm going to stay for three days*
> *Pour* for future planned duration.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Depuis vs Pendant vs Pour

| Word | Use | Tense | Example |
|------|-----|-------|---------|
| depuis | since, for (ongoing) | Present | J'habite ici depuis 2019 |
| pendant | during, for (completed) | Passé composé | J'ai travaillé pendant 2h |
| pour | for (planned) | Future / aller | Je pars pour une semaine |

💡 **Tip**: *Depuis* = still true. *Pendant* = finished. *Pour* = planned.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il y a** + duration = *ago* (Il est parti il y a trois ans)
- **en** + duration = *in* (J'ai fait ça en deux heures)
- **durant** — *during* (same as pendant)
- **depuis que** — *since* (Depuis que je te connais)
- **pendant que** — *while* (Pendant que tu dormais)

quizPairs.question: J'habite ici depuis cinq ans
quizPairs.answer: I've been living here for five years

quizPairs.question: Depuis quand tu le connais ?
quizPairs.answer: Since when have you known him?

quizPairs.question: Je vais rester pour trois jours
quizPairs.answer: I'm going to stay for three days

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 39: Comprehensive Review — All A2 Topics

Welcome back! This is your final review before graduation. We'll revisit: Imparfait, PC vs Imparfait, y/en/dont, Future Simple, subjunctive, negation, connaître/savoir, depuis/pendant/pour, and thematic vocabulary. One big dialogue to tie it all together.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases (Recap)

### Quand j'étais petit, je jouais au parc. Puis j'ai déménagé
> *When I was little, I played at the park. Then I moved*
> Imparfait + Passé composé.

### J'y vais. J'en ai deux. C'est le livre dont je t'ai parlé
> *I'm going there. I have two. It's the book I told you about*
> y, en, dont.

### Je viendrai. Il faut que tu viennes. Je n'ai rien mangé
> *I will come. You have to come. I didn't eat anything*
> Future, subjunctive, negation.

### Je connais Paris. Je sais nager. J'habite ici depuis cinq ans
> *I know Paris. I know how to swim. I've lived here for five years*
> Connaître/savoir, depuis.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: A2 in a Nutshell

- **Imparfait**: habits, descriptions, background
- **PC vs Imparfait**: events vs context
- **y, en, dont**: replace place, quantity, of which
- **Future**: futur simple + futur proche
- **Subjunctive**: il faut que, vouloir que, emotion, doubt
- **Negation**: ne...rien, personne, jamais, ni...ni
- **Duration**: depuis, pendant, pour

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **réviser** — *to review*
- **réussir** — *to succeed*
- **félicitations** — *congratulations*
- **à la prochaine** — *see you next time*

quizPairs.question: Quand j'étais petit, je jouais au parc. Puis j'ai déménagé
quizPairs.answer: When I was little, I played at the park. Then I moved

quizPairs.question: J'y vais. J'en ai deux. C'est le livre dont je t'ai parlé
quizPairs.answer: I'm going there. I have two. It's the book I told you about

quizPairs.question: Je viendrai. Il faut que tu viennes. Je n'ai rien mangé
quizPairs.answer: I will come. You have to come. I didn't eat anything

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 40: 🎓 A2 Complete — Congratulations!

Welcome to Lesson 40 — your A2 graduation! You've completed the Elementary level. Today we celebrate your progress, recap your journey, and preview B1.

---

## 🎉 You Did It!

### What You Can Now Do in French (A2):

✅ **Tell stories in the past**: Imparfait + Passé composé, when to use each
✅ **Use key pronouns**: y, en, dont — naturally in conversation
✅ **Talk about the future**: Future Simple + Futur proche
✅ **Compare and choose**: Comparatives and superlatives
✅ **Express wishes & doubts**: Subjunctive (il faut que, vouloir que, emotion)
✅ **Point and possess**: Demonstratives (ce, cette) and possessives (le mien)
✅ **Negate precisely**: ne...rien, personne, jamais, ni...ni
✅ **Distinguish connaître/savoir**: People & places vs facts & skills
✅ **Handle duration**: depuis, pendant, pour
✅ **Navigate real life**: Airports, trains, renting, work, education, health, culture

### Key Milestones Reached:
- 40 A2 lessons completed 🏆
- 500+ vocabulary words (A1 + A2) 📚
- 25+ grammar concepts mastered 🔑
- Ready for B1 🚀

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: What's Next? (B1 Preview)

| Topic | What You'll Learn |
|-------|-------------------|
| **Subjunctive Extended** | More triggers, past subjunctive |
| **Conditional** | Si clauses, politeness (je voudrais) |
| **Passive Voice** | être + past participle |
| **Reported Speech** | Il a dit que... |
| **Advanced Pronouns** | Position, order, combinations |
| **Idiomatic Expressions** | Faire, avoir, être expressions |

---

## 🎁 Bonus: Your A2 Toolkit

### Keep Practicing:
- **Listen**: RFI, France 24, French podcasts
- **Read**: Le Monde, simple novels, bandes dessinées
- **Speak**: Tandem, iTalki, conversation clubs
- **Write**: Journal, emails, social media in French

### Motivational Phrases:
- **Je continue à progresser** — *I keep making progress*
- **Le subjonctif n'est plus un mystère** — *The subjunctive is no longer a mystery*
- **Je suis prêt(e) pour le B1** — *I'm ready for B1*
- **Félicitations !** — *Congratulations!*

🎓 **Bravo ! Vous avez terminé le niveau A2 !** 🎓

quizPairs.question: Le subjonctif n'est plus un mystère
quizPairs.answer: The subjunctive is no longer a mystery
