- **das Sprichwort** — *proverb*
- **die Redewendung** — *idiom*
- **ehren** — *to honour*
- **der Taler** — *dollar (archaic)*

quizPairs.question: Wer den Pfennig nicht ehrt, ist des Talers nicht wert.
quizPairs.answer: Who doesn't honour the penny isn't worth the dollar.

quizPairs.question: Der frühe Vogel fängt den Wurm.
quizPairs.answer: The early bird catches the worm.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 27: Regionale Ausdrücke und Dialekte

In this lesson you will recognise **regional expressions and dialects** in German-speaking areas.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Servus! (Bayern/Österreich)
> *Hello!/Goodbye!* (Bavarian/Austrian)

### Grüezi! (Schweiz)
> *Hello!* (Swiss)

### Moin! (Norddeutschland)
> *Hi!* (Northern Germany)

### Da haben wir den Salat!
> *There we have the mess!* (idiom)

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Regional

Grüße, regionale Begriffe, typische Wendungen aus D-A-CH.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **regional** — *regional*
- **der Gruß** — *greeting*
- **der Dialekt** — *dialect*
- **D-A-CH** — *Germany, Austria, Switzerland*

## Lesson 28: Kulturelle Referenzen

In this lesson you will understand **cultural references** in German: history, arts, traditions.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Das ist ein Faust-Thema.
> *That's a Faust theme.* (reference to Goethe)

### Wir haben Oktoberfest-Stimmung.
> *We have Oktoberfest mood.*

### Er zitiert Schiller.
> *He quotes Schiller.*

### Das erinnert an die Wende.
> *That reminds of the fall of the Wall.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Kultur

Literatur, Geschichte, Feiertage, Symbolik – implizite Referenzen verstehen.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **die Referenz** — *reference*
- **die Wende** — *the fall of the Wall (1989)*
- **zitieren** — *to quote*
- **erinnern an** — *to remind of*

## Lesson 29: Humor und Wortwitz

In this lesson you will appreciate **humour and wit** in German.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Das war ein Wortspiel.
> *That was a pun.*

### Der Witz beruht auf Doppeldeutigkeit.
> *The joke relies on ambiguity.*

### Schwarzer Humor ist nicht jedermanns Sache.
> *Black humour isn't everyone's thing.*

### Sie hat einen trockenen Humor.
> *She has dry humour.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Humor

Wortspiel, schwarzer Humor, trockener Humor, Situationskomik.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **der Humor** — *humour*
- **der Wortwitz** — *wit, pun*
- **schwarz** — *black*
- **trocken** — *dry*

## Lesson 30: Block 6 Review

Review of culture and expressions from lessons 26–29: Sprichwörter, regionale Ausdrücke, kulturelle Referenzen, Humor.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Übung macht den Meister.
> *Practice makes perfect.*

### Servus! / Grüezi! / Moin!
> *Regional greetings.*

### Das ist ein Faust-Thema.
> *That's a Faust theme.*

### Sie hat einen trockenen Humor.
> *She has dry humour.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Recap

Sprichwörter, regionale Ausdrücke, kulturelle Referenzen, Humor – Umgang mit Nuancen.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **das Sprichwort** — *proverb*
- **regional** — *regional*
- **die Referenz** — *reference*
- **der Humor** — *humour*

## Lesson 31: Dokumentensynthese

In this lesson you will produce **document synthesis**: summarising multiple sources.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Die Quellen lassen sich wie folgt zusammenfassen.
> *The sources can be summarised as follows.*

### Allen Texten gemeinsam ist die Kritik an...
> *Common to all texts is the criticism of...*

### Während Quelle A behauptet, vertritt Quelle B...
> *While source A claims, source B maintains...*

### Die Synthese zeigt widersprüchliche Positionen.
> *The synthesis reveals contradictory positions.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Synthese

Mehrere Quellen zusammenführen, Gemeinsamkeiten, Unterschiede, Widersprüche.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **die Synthese** — *synthesis*
- **die Quelle** — *source*
- **zusammenfassen** — *to summarise*
- **der Widerspruch** — *contradiction*

quizPairs.question: Die Quellen lassen sich wie folgt zusammenfassen.
quizPairs.answer: The sources can be summarised as follows.

quizPairs.question: Während Quelle A behauptet, vertritt Quelle B...
quizPairs.answer: While source A claims, source B maintains...

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 32: Essay schreiben

In this lesson you will write **academic essays** in German: structure, argumentation, style.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Die These lautet: ...
> *The thesis states: ...*

### Im Folgenden werde ich argumentieren, dass...
> *In the following I will argue that...*

### Daraus ergibt sich die Schlussfolgerung...
> *From this follows the conclusion...*

### Abschließend lässt sich festhalten...
> *In conclusion, it can be stated...*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Essay

These, Argumentation, Schlussfolgerung, akademischer Stil.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **der Essay** — *essay*
- **die These** — *thesis*
- **argumentieren** — *to argue*
- **die Schlussfolgerung** — *conclusion*

quizPairs.question: Im Folgenden werde ich argumentieren, dass...
quizPairs.answer: In the following I will argue that...

quizPairs.question: Abschließend lässt sich festhalten...
quizPairs.answer: In conclusion, it can be stated...

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 33: Präsentation und Vortrag

In this lesson you will deliver **presentations** in German: structure, rhetoric, Q&A.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ich komme nun zum Kernpunkt.
> *I now come to the main point.*

### Lassen Sie mich das veranschaulichen.
> *Let me illustrate that.*

### Gibt es Fragen aus dem Publikum?
> *Are there questions from the audience?*

### Ich fasse die wichtigsten Punkte zusammen.
> *I summarise the most important points.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Präsentation

Einstieg, Hauptteil, Schluss, Veranschaulichung, Fragerunde.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **die Präsentation** — *presentation*
- **veranschaulichen** — *to illustrate*
- **das Publikum** — *audience*
- **der Kernpunkt** — *main point*

quizPairs.question: Ich komme nun zum Kernpunkt.
quizPairs.answer: I now come to the main point.

quizPairs.question: Gibt es Fragen aus dem Publikum?
quizPairs.answer: Are there questions from the audience?

quizPairs.question: Ich fasse die wichtigsten Punkte zusammen.
quizPairs.answer: I summarise the most important points.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 34: Debatte und Argumentation

In this lesson you will participate in **debates** in German: pro/contra, rebuttal, moderation.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Dagegen spricht, dass...
> *Against this speaks the fact that...*

### Ich entgegne dem vorherigen Redner...
> *I object to the previous speaker...*

### Das Argument greift nicht.
> *The argument doesn't hold.*

### Wir sollten die Waage halten zwischen...
> *We should balance between...*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Debatte

Pro- und Contra-Argumente, Widerlegen, Moderieren, faire Diskussion.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **die Debatte** — *debate*
- **entgegnen** — *to object*
- **das Argument** — *argument*
- **greifen** — *to hold (of arguments)*

quizPairs.question: Dagegen spricht, dass...
quizPairs.answer: Against this speaks the fact that...

quizPairs.question: Ich entgegne dem vorherigen Redner...
quizPairs.answer: I object to the previous speaker...

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 35: Block 7 Review

Review of productive skills from lessons 31–34: Synthese, Essay, Präsentation, Debatte.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Die Quellen lassen sich wie folgt zusammenfassen.
> *The sources can be summarised as follows.*

### Die These lautet: ...
> *The thesis states: ...*

### Ich komme nun zum Kernpunkt.
> *I now come to the main point.*

### Dagegen spricht, dass...
> *Against this speaks the fact that...*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Recap

Synthese, Essay, Präsentation, Debatte – produktive Kompetenzen auf C2.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **die Synthese** — *synthesis*
- **die These** — *thesis*
- **der Kernpunkt** — *main point*
- **die Debatte** — *debate*

quizPairs.question: Die Quellen lassen sich wie folgt zusammenfassen.
quizPairs.answer: The sources can be summarised as follows.

quizPairs.question: Ich komme nun zum Kernpunkt.
quizPairs.answer: I now come to the main point.

quizPairs.question: Dagegen spricht, dass...
quizPairs.answer: Against this speaks the fact that...

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 36: Aussprache und Intonation

In this lesson you will refine **pronunciation and intonation** for near-native clarity.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Die Betonung liegt auf der zweiten Silbe.
> *The stress is on the second syllable.*

### Der Satzakzent markiert die Fokusinformation.
> *Sentence stress marks the focus information.*

### Die Intonation signalisiert Frage vs. Aussage.
> *Intonation signals question vs. statement.*

### Ch und ich-Laut: Buch, ich.
> *Ch-sounds: Buch, ich.* (phonetic distinction)

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Aussprache

Betonung, Satzakzent, Intonation, schwierige Laute (ch, r, ü, ö).

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **die Aussprache** — *pronunciation*
- **die Intonation** — *intonation*
- **die Betonung** — *stress*
- **die Silbe** — *syllable*

quizPairs.question: Die Betonung liegt auf der zweiten Silbe.
quizPairs.answer: The stress is on the second syllable.

quizPairs.question: Der Satzakzent markiert die Fokusinformation.
quizPairs.answer: Sentence stress marks the focus information.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 37: Akzente und Nuancen

In this lesson you will distinguish **accents and nuances** in German: Austrian, Swiss, regional.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Der österreichische Akzent klingt weicher.
> *The Austrian accent sounds softer.*

### Das Schweizerdeutsche weicht stark ab.
> *Swiss German diverges strongly.*

### Die norddeutsche Aussprache ist klar.
> *Northern German pronunciation is clear.*

### Er spricht mit süddeutschem Einschlag.
> *He speaks with a Southern German influence.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Akzente

Österreichisch, Schweizerdeutsch, Norddeutsch, Süddeutsch – typische Merkmale.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **der Akzent** — *accent*
- **die Nuance** — *nuance*
- **weichen ab** — *to diverge*
- **der Einschlag** — *influence*

quizPairs.question: Er spricht mit süddeutschem Einschlag.
quizPairs.answer: He speaks with a Southern German influence.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 38: C2 Gesamtüberblick

In this lesson you will review the **entire C2 curriculum**: grammar, rhetoric, literature, discourse, production.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Sie beherrschen die Grammatik auf höchstem Niveau.
> *You master grammar at the highest level.*

### Rhetorische Mittel und Stilistik sind zentral.
> *Rhetorical devices and stylistics are central.*

### Sie können komplexe Texte analysieren und verfassen.
> *You can analyse and compose complex texts.*

### Herzlichen Glückwunsch zum C2-Niveau!
> *Congratulations on reaching C2 level!*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: C2 Überblick

Grammatik, Rhetorik, Literatur, Diskurse, produktive Fähigkeiten – alles im Überblick.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **der Überblick** — *overview*
- **beherrschen** — *to master*
- **verfassen** — *to compose*
- **das Niveau** — *level*

quizPairs.question: Sie beherrschen die Grammatik auf höchstem Niveau.
quizPairs.answer: You master grammar at the highest level.

quizPairs.question: Rhetorische Mittel und Stilistik sind zentral.
quizPairs.answer: Rhetorical devices and stylistics are central.

quizPairs.question: Sie können komplexe Texte analysieren und verfassen.
quizPairs.answer: You can analyse and compose complex texts.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 39: Mock C2 Prüfung

In this lesson you will complete a **mock C2 exam**: integrated tasks simulating Goethe/ÖSD C2.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Lesen Sie die Texte und fassen Sie zusammen.
> *Read the texts and summarise them.*

### Nehmen Sie Stellung zu der These.
> *Take a position on the thesis.*

### Halten Sie einen Kurzvortrag zu...
> *Give a short presentation on...*

### Diskutieren Sie die Vor- und Nachteile.
> *Discuss the pros and cons.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Mock Prüfung

Leseverstehen, Schreiben, Sprechen, Hörverstehen – C2-Format simulieren.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **die Prüfung** — *exam*
- **Stellung nehmen** — *to take a position*
- **der Kurzvortrag** — *short presentation*
- **die Vor- und Nachteile** — *pros and cons*

quizPairs.question: Lesen Sie die Texte und fassen Sie zusammen.
quizPairs.answer: Read the texts and summarise them.

quizPairs.question: Nehmen Sie Stellung zu der These.
quizPairs.answer: Take a position on the thesis.

quizPairs.question: Diskutieren Sie die Vor- und Nachteile.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 40: C2 Abschluss – Herzlichen Glückwunsch!

Congratulations! You have completed the **C2 German course**. You can now understand virtually everything and express yourself fluently and precisely.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Herzlichen Glückwunsch zum C2-Abschluss!
> *Congratulations on completing C2!*

### Sie haben das höchste Niveau erreicht.
> *You have reached the highest level.*

### Bleiben Sie am Ball – Übung hält frisch.
> *Stay with it – practice keeps you sharp.*

### Bis zum nächsten Mal! Viel Erfolg!
> *Until next time! Best of luck!*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: C2 Abschluss

Rückblick auf die 40 C2-Lektionen. Tipps für Weiterlernen und Praxis.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **der Abschluss** — *completion*
- **erreichen** — *to reach*
- **am Ball bleiben** — *to stay with it*
- **der Erfolg** — *success*

quizPairs.question: Sie haben das höchste Niveau erreicht.
quizPairs.answer: You have reached the highest level.

quizPairs.question: Bleiben Sie am Ball – Übung hält frisch.
quizPairs.answer: Stay with it – practice keeps you sharp.

quizPairs.question: Bis zum nächsten Mal! Viel Erfolg!
quizPairs.answer: Until next time! Best of luck!

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 1: Saluti e Come stai? — Greetings and How are you?

Welcome to **Coffee Break Italian**! In this first lesson you will learn how to greet people in Italian and ask how they are.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ciao
> *hi / bye (informal)*
> Very common. Works for both hello and goodbye.

### Buongiorno / Buon pomeriggio / Buonasera
> *good morning / good afternoon / good evening*
> Use in more formal situations or with strangers.

### Come stai?
> *How are you? (informal)*
> The formal version is *Come sta?*

### Sto bene, grazie.
> *I'm fine, thanks.*

### Non c'è male.
> *Not bad.*

### Così così.
> *So so.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: tu vs Lei

Italian has **informal** *tu* and **formal** *Lei* for "you".

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **Grazie.** — *thank you*
- **Prego.** — *you're welcome*
- **E tu? / E Lei?** — *And you?*
- **Bene!** — *well; good*

quizPairs.question: Buongiorno / Buon pomeriggio / Buonasera
quizPairs.answer: good morning / good afternoon / good evening

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 2: Presentarsi — Introducing Yourself

In this lesson you will learn how to say your name, ask for someone else's name and say where you are from.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Come ti chiami?
> *What is your name? (informal)*

### Mi chiamo ...
> *My name is ...*

### Piacere.
> *Nice to meet you.*

### Di dove sei?
> *Where are you from? (informal)*

### Sono di ...
> *I am from ...*

### Sono italiano / italiana.
> *I am Italian (m/f).*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Chiamarsi

*Chiamarsi* is a reflexive verb: *mi chiamo* (I call myself).

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il nome** — *name*
- **il cognome** — *surname*
- **venire** — *to come*

## Lesson 3: Numeri 1-20 e Età — Numbers 1-20 and Age

In this lesson you will learn to count from 1 to 20 and ask and tell someone's age.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### uno, due, tre
> *one, two, three*

### quattro, cinque, sei
> *four, five, six*

### Quanti anni hai?
> *How old are you? (informal)*

### Ho vent'anni.
> *I am twenty years old.*

### Quanto costa?
> *How much does it cost?*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Numeri

Italian numbers 1-20: uno, due, tre, quattro, cinque, sei, sette, otto, nove, dieci, undici, dodici, tredici, quattordici, quindici, sedici, diciassette, diciotto, diciannove, venti.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il numero** — *number*
- **gli anni** — *years*
- **avere** — *to have*

## Lesson 4: Giorni della settimana e Appuntamenti — Days and Plans

In this lesson you will learn the days of the week and how to make simple plans.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### lunedì, martedì, mercoledì
> *Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday*

### giovedì, venerdì, sabato, domenica
> *Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday*

### oggi / domani
> *today / tomorrow*

### Ci vediamo lunedì?
> *Shall we meet on Monday?*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Days

Days of the week are not capitalized in Italian.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **la settimana** — *week*
- **il giorno** — *day*
- **l'appuntamento** — *appointment*

## Lesson 5: Che ore sono? — Telling Time

In this lesson you will learn how to tell time and make appointments.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Che ore sono?
> *What time is it?*

### Sono le tre.
> *It's three o'clock.*

### È l'una.
> *It's one o'clock.*

### A che ora?
> *At what time?*

### alle otto / all'una
> *at eight / at one*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Time

Use *è* for 1:00, *sono* for 2-12. Le for hours: *sono le cinque*.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **l'ora** — *hour*
- **mezzogiorno / mezzanotte** — *noon / midnight*
- **e un quarto** — *quarter past*

## Lesson 6: Verbi e Routine — Verbs and Daily Routine

In this lesson you will learn essential verbs and how to talk about your daily routine.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### parlare / mangiare / lavorare
> *to speak / to eat / to work*

### Io parlo italiano.
> *I speak Italian.*

### Tu mangi a mezzogiorno.
> *You eat at noon.*

### abitare / vivere
> *to live (in a place)*

### Abito a Roma.
> *I live in Rome.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: -are verbs

Regular -are: parl-o, parl-i, parl-a, parl-iamo, parl-ate, parl-ano.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il verbo** — *verb*
- **la routine** — *routine*
- **svegliarsi** — *to wake up*

quizPairs.question: parlare / mangiare / lavorare
quizPairs.answer: to speak / to eat / to work

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 7: Al bar — Food and Ordering

In this lesson you will learn food vocabulary and how to order at a café.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Un caffè, per favore.
> *A coffee, please.*

### Vorrei un cappuccino.
> *I would like a cappuccino.*

### Quanto costa?
> *How much does it cost?*

### Il conto, per favore.
> *The bill, please.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Ordering

Use *vorrei* (I would like) for polite requests.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il caffè** — *coffee*
- **la pasta** — *pasta*
- **per favore** — *please*

## Lesson 8: La famiglia — Family and Describing People

In this lesson you will learn family vocabulary and basic adjectives.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### la famiglia
> *the family*

### mio padre / mia madre
> *my father / my mother*

### mio fratello / mia sorella
> *my brother / my sister*

### È alto / È bassa.
> *He is tall. / She is short.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Possessives

Mio/mia, tuo/tua agree with the noun: *mio fratello*, *mia sorella*.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il padre** — *father*
- **la madre** — *mother*
- **grande** — *big*
- **piccolo** — *small*

quizPairs.question: È alto / È bassa.
quizPairs.answer: He is tall. / She is short.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 9: Fare acquisti e Prezzi — Shopping and Prices

In this lesson you will learn numbers 20-100 and shopping phrases.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### venti, trenta, quaranta, cinquanta
> *20, 30, 40, 50*

### sessanta, settanta, ottanta, novanta, cento
> *60, 70, 80, 90, 100*

### Cerco ...
> *I'm looking for ...*

### Quanto costa questo?
> *How much does this cost?*

### Lo prendo.
> *I'll take it.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Numbers

Italian numbers 20-100 follow a regular pattern.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **comprare** — *to buy*
- **il negozio** — *shop*
- **cercare** — *to look for*

## Lesson 10: Ripasso e Conversazioni — Review and Simple Conversations

Review of key phrases from Lessons 1-9.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ciao! Come stai?
> *Hi! How are you?*

### Mi chiamo ... Di dove sei?
> *My name is ... Where are you from?*

### Ci vediamo lunedì?
> *Shall we meet on Monday?*

### Vorrei un caffè, per favore.
> *I would like a coffee, please.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Recap

Greetings, introductions, time, numbers, food, family, shopping.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **ripasso** — *review*
- **la conversazione** — *conversation*
- **arrivederci** — *goodbye*

quizPairs.question: Mi chiamo ... Di dove sei?
quizPairs.answer: My name is ... Where are you from?

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 11: Luoghi in città e Indicazioni — Places and Directions

In this lesson you will learn places in the city and how to ask for directions.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Dov'è ... ?
> *Where is ...?*

### a sinistra / a destra / diritto
> *to the left / to the right / straight*

### la stazione / la piazza / il parco
> *the station / the square / the park*

### vicino a / lontano da
> *near / far from*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Directions

*Dov'è* = dove + è (where is). *Diritto* = straight ahead.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **la città** — *city*
- **la strada** — *street*
- **l'indirizzo** — *address*

quizPairs.question: la stazione / la piazza / il parco
quizPairs.answer: the station / the square / the park

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 12: Il tempo e le stagioni — Weather and Seasons

In this lesson you will learn weather vocabulary and the seasons.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Che tempo fa?
> *What's the weather like?*

### Fa bel tempo / Fa brutto tempo.
> *The weather is nice / bad.*

### piove / nevica
> *it rains / it snows*

### primavera, estate, autunno, inverno
> *spring, summer, autumn, winter*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Weather

Use *fa* with weather: *fa caldo*, *fa freddo*.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il tempo** — *weather*
- **sole** — *sun*
- **la stagione** — *season*

quizPairs.question: Fa bel tempo / Fa brutto tempo.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 13: Hobby e tempo libero — Hobbies and Free Time

In this lesson you will learn to talk about hobbies and free time.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Cosa ti piace fare?
> *What do you like to do?*

### Mi piace leggere / nuotare.
> *I like to read / swim.*

### nel tempo libero
> *in my free time*

### suonare / viaggiare / cucinare
> *to play (instrument) / to travel / to cook*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Piacere

*Mi piace* + infinitive: I like to... *Mi piacciono* + plural noun.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **l'hobby** — *hobby*
- **il tempo libero** — *free time*
- **leggere** — *to read*

quizPairs.question: Cosa ti piace fare?
quizPairs.answer: What do you like to do?

quizPairs.question: suonare / viaggiare / cucinare
quizPairs.answer: to play (instrument) / to travel / to cook

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 14: Al ristorante — At the Restaurant

In this lesson you will learn how to order at a restaurant.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Un tavolo per due, per favore.
> *A table for two, please.*

### Il menu, per favore.
> *The menu, please.*

### Vorrei la pasta alla carbonara.
> *I would like the carbonara pasta.*

### L'acqua, per favore.
> *Water, please.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Ordering

*Vorrei* = I would like. *Per favore* = please.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il ristorante** — *restaurant*
- **il menu** — *menu*
- **il cameriere** — *waiter*

quizPairs.question: Un tavolo per due, per favore.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 15: Ripasso — Lessons 11-14

Review of places, weather, hobbies, and restaurant.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Dov'è la stazione?
### Che tempo fa?
### Mi piace viaggiare.
### Un tavolo per due.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Recap

Directions, weather, hobbies, restaurant.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **ripassare** — *to review*
- **praticare** — *to practice*

## Lesson 16: Colori e vestiti — Colors and Clothing

In this lesson you will learn colors and clothing vocabulary.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### rosso, blu, verde, giallo
> *red, blue, green, yellow*

### Che taglia porti?
> *What size do you wear?*

### Mi sta bene.
> *It fits me.*

### costa troppo
> *it costs too much*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Colors

Adjectives agree: *la camicia rossa*, *il vestito rosso*.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il colore** — *color*
- **il vestito** — *dress*
- **la taglia** — *size*

## Lesson 17: Corpo e salute — Body and Health

In this lesson you will learn body parts and basic health vocabulary.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ho mal di testa.
> *I have a headache.*

### Mi fa male il braccio.
> *My arm hurts.*

### Dove si trova la farmacia?
> *Where is the pharmacy?*

### Ho bisogno di ...
> *I need ...*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Health

*Mal di* + body part = ache. *Mi fa male* = it hurts me.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **la testa** — *head*
- **la farmacia** — *pharmacy*
- **il dolore** — *pain*

## Lesson 18: Viaggi e trasporti — Travel and Transportation

In this lesson you will learn travel and transportation vocabulary.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Dove andiamo?
> *Where are we going?*

### Come arrivo a ...?
> *How do I get to ...?*

### in treno / in autobus / in macchina
> *by train / by bus / by car*

### Un biglietto per Roma, per favore.
> *A ticket to Rome, please.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Transport

*In* + means: *in aereo* (by plane), *in bicicletta* (by bike).

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il treno** — *train*
- **il biglietto** — *ticket*
- **viaggiare** — *to travel*

quizPairs.question: Un biglietto per Roma, per favore.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 19: Emozioni e sentimenti — Emotions and Feelings

In this lesson you will learn to talk about emotions and feelings.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Sono felice / triste.
> *I am happy / sad.*

### Ho paura.
> *I am afraid.*

### Sono stanco / stanca.
> *I am tired (m/f).*

### Mi sento bene.
> *I feel well.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Emotions

*Sono* + adjective. *Stanca* for feminine.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **la felicità** — *happiness*
- **la paura** — *fear*
- **sentire** — *to feel*

## Lesson 20: Ripasso — Lessons 16-19

Review of colors, health, travel, emotions.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Che taglia porti?
### Ho mal di testa.
### Un biglietto per Roma.
### Sono felice.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Recap

Clothing, body, transport, emotions.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **ripasso** — *review*
- **la lezione** — *lesson*

## Lesson 21: Passato prossimo con avere — Introduction to Past Tense

In this lesson you will learn the passato prossimo with avere.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ho mangiato una pizza.
> *I ate a pizza.*

### Hai visto il film?
> *Did you see the film?*

### Abbiamo parlato ieri.
> *We spoke yesterday.*

### avere + participio passato
> *have + past participle*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Passato prossimo

Ho/ha/hanno + past participle (-ato, -uto, -ito for regular verbs).

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **ieri** — *yesterday*
- **il participio passato** — *past participle*
- **mangiare** — *to eat*

## Lesson 22: Passato prossimo — Irregular Verbs

In this lesson you will practice passato prossimo with common irregular verbs.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Sono andato / andata a casa.
> *I went home (m/f).*

### È venuto ieri.
> *He came yesterday.*

### Ho fatto colazione.
> *I had breakfast.*

### Ha detto che sì.
> *He said yes.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Essere or Avere

Verbs of motion use *essere*: andare, venire, partire. Past participle agrees with subject.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **andare** — *to go*
- **fare** — *to do/make*
- **dire** — *to say*

## Lesson 23: Futuro prossimo e Futuro semplice — Near and Simple Future

In this lesson you will learn to talk about the future.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Stasera andrò al cinema.
> *Tonight I will go to the cinema.*

### Domani farò una passeggiata.
> *Tomorrow I will take a walk.*

### Verrà la settimana prossima.
> *He will come next week.*

### Mangerò fuori.
> *I will eat out.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Futuro

Futuro semplice: -erò, -erai, -erà. Irregular: andrò, farò, vedrò.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **domani** — *tomorrow*
- **la settimana prossima** — *next week*
- **il futuro** — *future*

quizPairs.question: Stasera andrò al cinema.
quizPairs.answer: Tonight I will go to the cinema.

quizPairs.question: Domani farò una passeggiata.
quizPairs.answer: Tomorrow I will take a walk.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 24: Negazione — Beyond Non

In this lesson you will learn negation beyond non.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Non ho visto nessuno.
> *I didn't see anyone.*

### Non c'è niente.
> *There is nothing.*

### Non... mai
> *never*

### Non... più
> *no longer*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Negation

Non + verb. Nessuno, niente, mai, più follow the verb.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **nessuno** — *no one*
- **niente** — *nothing*
- **mai** — *never*

## Lesson 25: Pronomi diretti — Direct Object Pronouns

In this lesson you will learn direct object pronouns: lo, la, li, le.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Lo vedo.
> *I see him/it (masc).*

### La conosco.
> *I know her/it (fem).*

### Li ho chiamati.
> *I called them (masc).*

### Le prendo.
> *I'll take them (fem).*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Direct pronouns

lo/la (singular), li/le (plural). Before the verb except with infinitive.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **vedere** — *to see*
- **conoscere** — *to know*
- **il pronome** — *pronoun*

## Lesson 26: Casa e mobili — Home and Furniture

In this lesson you will learn vocabulary for the home and furniture.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Abito in un appartamento.
> *I live in an apartment.*

### La cucina è grande.
> *The kitchen is big.*

### C'è un divano in salotto.
> *There is a sofa in the living room.*

### Dov'è il bagno?
> *Where is the bathroom?*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Home

cucina, camera da letto, bagno, salotto, soggiorno.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **la casa** — *house*
- **la camera** — *room*
- **il divano** — *sofa*

quizPairs.question: C'è un divano in salotto.
quizPairs.answer: There is a sofa in the living room.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 27: Professioni e lavoro — Professions and Work

In this lesson you will learn professions and work vocabulary.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Di cosa ti occupi?
> *What do you do for work?*

### Sono insegnante.
> *I am a teacher.*

### Lavoro in ufficio.
> *I work in an office.*

### Il mio capo è molto bravo.
> *My boss is very good.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Professions

No article with profession after *essere*: Sono dottore.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **il lavoro** — *work*
- **la professione** — *profession*
- **l'ufficio** — *office*

quizPairs.question: Di cosa ti occupi?
quizPairs.answer: What do you do for work?

quizPairs.question: Il mio capo è molto bravo.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 28: Pasti e abitudini alimentari — Meals and Eating Habits

In this lesson you will learn about meals and eating habits.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Faccio colazione alle otto.
> *I have breakfast at eight.*

### A che ora pranzi?
> *What time do you have lunch?*

### Ceniamo insieme stasera?
> *Shall we have dinner together tonight?*

### Mangio sempre la pizza il venerdì.
> *I always eat pizza on Fridays.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Meals

colazione (breakfast), pranzo (lunch), cena (dinner).

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **la colazione** — *breakfast*
- **il pranzo** — *lunch*
- **la cena** — *dinner*

## Lesson 29: Shopping e taglie — Shopping and Sizes

In this lesson you will learn shopping vocabulary and sizes.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Questa maglietta è troppo piccola.
> *This T-shirt is too small.*

### Ha una taglia più grande?
> *Do you have a larger size?*

### Posso provarla?
> *Can I try it on?*

### Quanto costa in totale?
> *How much does it cost in total?*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Sizes

piccolo, medio, grande. Taglia S, M, L.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **la taglia** — *size*
- **provare** — *to try*
- **in totale** — *in total*

quizPairs.question: Ha una taglia più grande?
quizPairs.answer: Do you have a larger size?

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 30: Ripasso — Lessons 21-29

Review of passato prossimo, futuro, negation, pronouns, home, work, meals, shopping.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ho mangiato / Sono andato
### Stasera andrò
### Non ho visto nessuno
### Lo vedo
### Abito in un appartamento

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Recap

Past, future, negation, pronouns, home, work, meals, shopping.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **ripasso** — *review*
- **praticare** — *to practice*

## Lesson 31: Viaggi e hotel — Travel and Hotels

In this lesson you will learn travel and hotel vocabulary.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Vorrei prenotare una camera.
> *I would like to book a room.*

### Per quanti notti?
> *For how many nights?*

### La colazione è inclusa?
> *Is breakfast included?*

### Dov'è la reception?
> *Where is the reception?*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Hotels

prenotare, camera singola/doppia, check-in, check-out.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **l'hotel** — *hotel*
- **la camera** — *room*
- **prenotare** — *to book*

## Lesson 32: Ripasso — Lessons 21-29 (Recap)

Review of passato prossimo, futuro, negation, pronouns.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ho mangiato / Sono andato
### Domani andrò
### Non c'è niente
### Lo prendo

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Recap

Past tense, future, negation, direct pronouns.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **ripasso** — *review*
- **ricordare** — *to remember*

## Lesson 33: Emozioni e relazioni — Emotions and Relationships

In this lesson you will learn about emotions and relationships.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ti voglio bene.
> *I love you (to family/friends).*

### Mi manchi.
> *I miss you.*

### Siamo amici da anni.
> *We have been friends for years.*

### Ci vediamo presto!
> *See you soon!*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Relationships

amico, fidanzato, marito, moglie. Ti voglio bene vs Ti amo.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **l'amico** — *friend*
- **la relazione** — *relationship*
- **mancare** — *to miss*

quizPairs.question: Siamo amici da anni.
quizPairs.answer: We have been friends for years.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 34: Cultura italiana e tradizioni — Italian Culture and Traditions

In this lesson you will learn about Italian culture and traditions.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Il pranzo della domenica
> *Sunday lunch*

### La famiglia è molto importante.
> *Family is very important.*

### Festeggiamo il Natale insieme.
> *We celebrate Christmas together.*

### Buon appetito!
> *Enjoy your meal!*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Culture

Cibo, famiglia, feste, tradizioni regionali.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **la tradizione** — *tradition*
- **la festa** — *holiday/party*
- **il Natale** — *Christmas*

## Lesson 35: Pre-ripasso finale — Pre-Final Review

Review and conversation practice before the final lessons.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ciao! Come stai? Mi chiamo...
### Che ore sono? Ci vediamo...
### Ho mangiato. Domani andrò.
### Vorrei un caffè. Grazie!

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Recap

All A1 topics: greetings, time, past, future, food, travel, culture.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **ripasso** — *review*
- **praticare** — *to practice*
- **la conversazione** — *conversation*

## Lesson 36: Pronomi indiretti — Indirect Object Pronouns

In this lesson you will learn indirect object pronouns: mi, ti, gli, le, ci, vi.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Mi piace la pizza.
> *I like pizza.*

### Ti do il libro.
> *I give you the book.*

### Gli ho detto di venire.
> *I told him to come.*

### Le scrivo una lettera.
> *I write her a letter.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Indirect pronouns

mi, ti, gli/le, ci, vi. Before the verb.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **dare** — *to give*
- **scrivere** — *to write*
- **dire** — *to say*

## Lesson 37: Pronomi relativi semplici — Simple Relative Clauses

In this lesson you will learn simple relative pronouns: che, dove.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Il libro che leggo
> *The book that I read*

### La città dove abito
> *The city where I live*

### L'uomo che parla
> *The man who speaks*

### Il ragazzo che conosco
> *The boy whom I know*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Che and dove

Che = who/whom/that. Dove = where.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **che** — *that/who/which*
- **dove** — *where*
- **il pronome relativo** — *relative pronoun*

## Lesson 38: Richieste educate — Polite Requests

In this lesson you will learn polite requests and the conditional for politeness.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Potrebbe aiutarmi?
> *Could you help me?*

### Vorrei ... per favore.
> *I would like ... please.*

### Mi scusi, dov'è...?
> *Excuse me, where is...?*

### Per favore / Per piacere
> *Please*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Politeness

Use conditional (potrebbe, vorrei) and per favore for polite requests.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **aiutare** — *to help*
- **scusare** — *to excuse*
- **per favore** — *please*

## Lesson 39: Ripasso completo — Final Comprehensive Review

Review of all A1 topics before graduation.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

Greetings, introductions, numbers, time, verbs, food, family, shopping, directions, weather, hobbies, restaurant, past, future, negation, pronouns, home, work, meals, travel, culture.

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: A1 Complete

All grammar and vocabulary from Lessons 1-38.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **ripasso** — *review*
- **completo** — *complete*
- **complimenti** — *congratulations*

## Lesson 40: A1 Complete — Complimenti!

Congratulations! You have completed the **A1 Italian course**. You can now greet people, introduce yourself, talk about daily life, use the past and future, and handle basic situations.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Complimenti! Hai completato il livello A1!
> *Congratulations! You have completed A1 level!*

### Sei pronto per l'A2!
> *You are ready for A2!*

### Continuiamo! A presto!
> *Let's continue! See you soon!*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: A1 Achievement

Presente, passato prossimo, futuro, pronomi, lessico essenziale.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **complimenti** — *congratulations*
- **completare** — *to complete*
- **il livello** — *level*
- **a presto** — *see you soon*

quizPairs.question: Complimenti! Hai completato il livello A1!
quizPairs.answer: Congratulations! You have completed A1 level!

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 1: Infanzia e abitudini — Introduction to Imperfetto

In this lesson you will learn the imperfetto for habits and ongoing actions in the past.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Da bambino andavo al mare.
> *As a child I used to go to the seaside.*

### Mangiavo sempre la pasta.
> *I always ate pasta.*

### Era bello.
> *It was beautiful.*

### L'imperfetto
> *the imperfect tense*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Imperfetto

-avo, -evo, -ivo. For habits, descriptions, ongoing actions in the past.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **l'infanzia** — *childhood*
- **l'abitudine** — *habit*
- **da bambino** — *as a child*

quizPairs.question: Da bambino andavo al mare.
quizPairs.answer: As a child I used to go to the seaside.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 2: Descrivere il passato — Imperfetto con essere e avere

In this lesson you will learn imperfetto with essere and avere.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ero a casa.
> *I was at home.*

### Avevo fame.
> *I was hungry.*

### Era bella.
> *She was beautiful.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Imperfetto

Ero, eri, era; avevo, avevi, aveva. Descriptions and states in the past.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **essere** — *to be*
- **avere** — *to have*
- **la fame** — *hunger*

## Lesson 3: Abitudini nel passato — Imperfetto for Routines

In this lesson you will use imperfetto for habitual past actions.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Tutti i giorni bevevo caffè.
> *Every day I used to drink coffee.*

### Da piccolo giocavo a calcio.
> *As a child I used to play football.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight: Habits

Imperfetto = used to, would (habitual). -avo, -evo, -ivo.

---

## 🎁 Bonus Vocabulary

- **tutti i giorni** — *every day*
- **giocare** — *to play*
- **bere** — *to drink*

quizPairs.question: Tutti i giorni bevevo caffè.
quizPairs.answer: Every day I used to drink coffee.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 4: Tempo e atmosfera — Imperfetto with Weather

Imperfetto with weather and atmosphere: Faceva bel tempo, Era estate.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Faceva bel tempo.
> *The weather was nice.*

### Era estate.
> *It was summer.*

### C'era il sole.
> *There was sun.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight

Faceva, c'era, era for past weather and atmosphere.

---

## 🎁 Bonus

- **il tempo** — *weather*
- **l'estate** — *summer*
- **il sole** — *sun*

## Lesson 5: Ripasso Imperfetto

Review of imperfetto: formation and usage.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Andavo, mangiavo, ero.
> *I used to go, eat, was.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight

Imperfetto consolidation.

---

## 🎁 Bonus

- **ripasso** — *review*
- **l'imperfetto** — *imperfect*

quizPairs.question: Andavo, mangiavo, ero
quizPairs.answer: I used to go, eat, was

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 6: Passato prossimo vs Imperfetto — When to Use Each

When to use passato prossimo vs imperfetto.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Mentre mangiavo è arrivato.
> *While I was eating he arrived.*

### Ieri ho mangiato. Da bambino mangiavo.
> *Yesterday I ate. As a child I used to eat.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight

PP = completed event. Imperfetto = background, habit, description.

---

## 🎁 Bonus

- **mentre** — *while*
- **arrivare** — *to arrive*

quizPairs.question: Mentre mangiavo è arrivato.
quizPairs.answer: While I was eating he arrived.

quizPairs.question: Ieri ho mangiato. Da bambino mangiavo.
quizPairs.answer: Yesterday I ate. As a child I used to eat.

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 7: Raccontare una storia — Telling a Story

Passato prossimo for events + imperfetto for context.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Stavo cucinando quando ha suonato.
> *I was cooking when the doorbell rang.*

### Leggevo quando è entrato.
> *I was reading when he came in.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight

Imperfetto (background) + passato prossimo (event).

---

## 🎁 Bonus

- **cucinare** — *to cook*
- **suonare** — *to ring*
- **entrare** — *to enter*

## Lesson 8: Azioni interrotte — Interrupted Actions

Imperfetto + quando + passato prossimo for interrupted actions.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Leggevo quando è entrato.
> *I was reading when he came in.*

### Dormivo quando ha telefonato.
> *I was sleeping when he called.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight

Background (imperfetto) interrupted by event (passato prossimo).

---

## 🎁 Bonus

- **dormire** — *to sleep*
- **telefonare** — *to call*
- **quando** — *when*

## Lesson 9: Opinioni nel passato — Pensavo che...

Opinions and descriptions in the past. Pensavo che + imperfetto/congiuntivo.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Pensavo che fosse bello.
> *I thought it was beautiful.*

### Credevo che venisse.
> *I believed he would come.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight

Pensavo, credevo + che + congiuntivo imperfetto (intro).

---

## 🎁 Bonus

- **pensare** — *to think*
- **credere** — *to believe*
- **fosse** — *was (subj)*

## Lesson 10: Ripasso tempi passati

Review of mixed past tenses.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ho fatto e facevo.
> *I did and I used to do.*

lessons.markdown:
## Lesson 11: Pronome ci — There, Replacing Place

The pronoun ci: replacing a+place, there.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ci siamo andati ieri.
> *We went there yesterday.*

### Ci vado domani.
> *I'll go there tomorrow.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight

ci = there, or a+place (andare a Roma → ci vado).

---

## 🎁 Bonus

- **ci** — *there*
- **andare** — *to go*
- **ieri** — *yesterday*

## Lesson 12: Pronome ne — With Quantities

The pronoun ne with quantities and partitives.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ne voglio due.
> *I want two of them.*

### Quanti ne vuoi?
> *How many do you want?*

### Ce n'è abbastanza.
> *There is enough.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight

ne = of it/them. Replaces di+noun with quantities.

---

## 🎁 Bonus

- **ne** — *of it/them*
- **abbastanza** — *enough*
- **quanto** — *how much*

## Lesson 13: ci e ne insieme

Using ci and ne together.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### Ce ne sono tre.
> *There are three of them.*

### Ce n'è un po'.
> *There is a little.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight

ce + ne. Ce ne = there of them. Combined form.

---

## 🎁 Bonus

- **ce ne** — *there of them*
- **un po'** — *a little*
- **essere** — *to be*

## Lesson 14: Pronome relativo cui

The relative pronoun cui: of which, to whom, in which.

---

## 🔤 Key Phrases

### La città in cui vivo.
> *The city in which I live.*

### L'uomo di cui parlo.
> *The man of whom I speak.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight

cui = whom/which with prepositions. In cui, di cui, a cui.

---

## 🎁 Bonus

- **cui** — *whom/which*
- **vivere** — *to live*
- **parlare** — *to speak*

quizPairs.question: La città in cui vivo.
quizPairs.answer: The city in which I live.

quizPairs.question: L'uomo di cui parlo.
quizPairs.answer: The man of whom I speak.

### Andrò domani.
> *I will go tomorrow.*

### Parleremo di questo.
> *We will talk about this.*

---

## 🔦 Spotlight

-erò, -erai, -erà. -are → -er, -ere → -er, -ire → -ir.

- **andare** — *to go*

- **domani** — *tomorrow*

> *I will be there.*

> *I will have time.*

## 🔦 Spotlight

essere→sarò, avere→avrò, venire→verrò, andare→andrò.

- **grande** — *big*
